auth.title: Involuntary session-expiry (401) path leaves the previous user's trips/expenses/days/budgets in STATE and re-persists them to localStorage — shared-device privacy leak
auth.rootCause: The deliberate logout() in pages/profile.ts:70-101 was carefully hardened (R2 / SY7 / SY9 / R8-B3) to null out user, activeTripId, trips, archivedTrips, expenses, tripDays, budgets, activities, photos, notifications, savedFormats, categories AND clearOutbox(). But the INVOLUNTARY teardown path — apiFetch()'s 401 branch (core.ts:269-284) — only calls clearAuthToken() + sets STATE.user=null + emit. clearAuthToken() (core.ts:82-157) removes the localStorage blob and resets notifications/draftExpense/geminiApiKey/settlements/achievements/lastImportBatch/preferences/rateMode/insightCurrency, but it does NOT clear trips/archivedTrips/expenses/tripDays/budgets/activities/photos/savedFormats/categories or the outbox. Because saveState (subscribed to state:changed, state.ts:438) serializes the WHOLE STATE object 250 ms after the emit, the just-removed blob is immediately repopulated with the previous user's data. The SW cache IS cleared (CLEAR_API_CACHE / CLEAR_USER are posted from clearAuthToken), which is why this gap was missed — only the in-memory + localStorage STATE leak remains.

auth.title: isCreator omitted from /api/auth/google login response → Creator tab/card invisible to non-admin Creators until a full page reload
auth.rootCause: The login response in /api/auth/google (auth.py:259-278) builds the `user` object WITHOUT an `isCreator` field, whereas /api/user-status (the boot/reload probe, auth.py:64-84) computes and returns `isCreator`. handleGoogleLogin sets `STATE.user = data.user` (auth.ts:58), so after a fresh login STATE.user.isCreator is undefined. isCreatorUser() = `!!STATE.user?.isCreator || isAdminUser()` (Settings.tsx:78-80) is therefore false for a non-admin creator. Neither pullFromServer nor /api/data re-hydrate STATE.user or carry is_creator, so the value stays undefined until the next boot calls restoreSession()→/api/user-status, which is the only place isCreator is populated.

auth.title: auth_sessions rows are never reaped once their JWT expires (only revoked rows are swept) → Sessions UI lists phantom dead devices and the table grows unbounded
auth.rootCause: issue_token()→_create_session() inserts a NEW auth_sessions row on every single login (auth.py:176-216) and nothing ever deletes a session purely because its JWT expired. list_sessions (auth.py:357-363) selects `WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL` with NO created_at/expiry filter, so every never-revoked row is returned regardless of whether its token is still valid. The background sweep in _cleanup_feed_orphans (main.py:795-798) only deletes rows where `revoked_at IS NOT NULL AND revoked_at < now-30d` — it never touches expired-but-unrevoked rows. (Most logouts revoke just the current session and most expirations revoke nothing, so the un-revoked population dominates.)

auth.title: Settings → Sessions panel is entirely hardcoded English (no t() calls) — es/fr/pt users see an untranslated device-management screen
auth.rootCause: Sessions.tsx self-documents the gap at lines 118-120 ('keys for these strings can be added to the locale tables once the SessionsView ships — for now we hard-code English'). All visible strings are string literals rather than t(...) lookups, and no corresponding keys exist in locales/{en,es,fr,pt}.ts. The relative-time helper _formatRelativeTime DOES use the i18n table (feed.relTime*), which makes the surrounding hardcoded strings inconsistent.

auth.title: Sessions UI device-label re-parser (and its docstring) assume a raw User-Agent, but the server now stores a pre-summarized label — the regex parse is dead code that survives only by accidental fallback
auth.rootCause: The server changed (R2 audit, auth.py:135-173) to store a summarized 'Browser on OS' string via _summarize_ua() in _create_session (auth.py:192), specifically to avoid leaking the full UA fingerprint. The client (_deviceLabel) and the misc.ts doc comment were never updated to match — they still treat deviceLabel as a verbatim UA. Because the summary contains no 'Browser/version' token, the parse produces browser='' and os='Unknown' and returns the raw summary, so it only works by coincidence; any future change to the summary format (or a label like 'Opera on Linux' that contains an OS keyword) would render a wrong/garbled label.

auth.title: initGoogleLogin() renders the GIS button into a #googleBtnContainer element that does not exist anywhere — dead/misleading boot code
auth.rootCause: initGoogleLogin (auth.ts:160-171) polls for the GIS SDK and then does `const container = document.getElementById('googleBtnContainer'); if (container) renderButton(...)`. The only login button container that exists is #loginWallBtnContainer, created by LoginWall.tsx:141. So the container lookup always returns null and the renderButton never runs from this path; initGoogleLogin's only live effect is the early ensureGsiInitialized() callback wiring. The dead branch reads as if a second button mount point exists.

trips.title: Failed trip DELETE silently swallowed — trip vanishes then reappears on next poll with no error
trips.rootCause: deleteTrip() -> _delete() (core.ts:302) calls apiFetch(...).catch(e=>console.error). apiFetch only THROWS on network-level failures (it returns the Response for any HTTP status, including 4xx/5xx). So a 500/403 is a resolved promise with res.ok=false. Neither deleteActiveTrip (trip-controls.ts:200-204) nor deleteArchivedTrip (handlers.ts:135-139) ever inspects res.ok — they only `catch` (which never fires for an HTTP error) and then navigate. Meanwhile delete_trip server-side only writes the trip_deletes tombstone INSIDE the transaction (trips.py:443), so a failed delete leaves no tombstone and the row persists. pullFromServer does `STATE.trips = allTrips.filter(...)` (api.ts:183) — a full replace from the server — so the still-existing trip is re-added. The optimistic local removal is undone with zero user feedback.

trips.title: Failed trip CREATE (daily cap 429 / 500) is silently swallowed; new trip is lost on next poll
trips.rootCause: openNewTripModal’s submit does `try { await upsertTrip(newTrip); await Promise.all(scaffolded.map(upsertDay)) } catch { /* outbox retries */ } navigate('home')`. upsertTrip -> _upsertWithUpdatedAt returns {ok:false,status:429|500,...} WITHOUT throwing for any HTTP response (api.ts:419 returns the envelope; only network errors throw). So the catch never fires and the return value is ignored. The outbox is only populated in apiFetch’s network-failure catch (core.ts:236-256), so a 429/500 is NOT queued for retry. On the next pull, STATE.trips is fully replaced from the server (api.ts:183), which has no such trip → it vanishes. Note the AI-plan flow explicitly handles userCapHit (useAiPlan.ts:370) but the trip-create flow does not.

trips.title: Cloned trip's marked places keep the SOURCE trip's dayId → wishlist pins become invisible on every day filter
trips.rootCause: _clone_trip_attempt copies src['marked_places_json'] VERBATIM (trips.py:1309) while generating brand-new day ids for the clone’s trip_days. So each cloned marked place still carries dayId pointing at a SOURCE-trip day that does not exist in the clone. In todoMarkers.ts the day filter does: if (!selectedIsAnchor && place.dayId && place.dayId !== selectedDayId) continue (line 183) and if (!selectedIsAnchor && !place.dayId) continue (line 186). A place with a dangling dayId never equals any real day in the clone, so it’s skipped on every numbered-day view, AND because it HAS a dayId it’s skipped on the Anchor view too → invisible everywhere. The template snapshot path explicitly strips dayId/timeOfDay for exactly this reason (templates.py:106-107); the clone path never got the same treatment.

trips.title: No cap on trip date range → unbounded day scaffolding; days beyond 999 silently rejected by the server
trips.rootCause: mountDateRangePicker configures flatpickr in range mode with NO maxDate and no max-span (dateRangePicker.ts:89-101). _scaffoldTripDays loops day-by-day from start to end with no upper bound (_shared.ts:109). The create handler then does Promise.all(scaffolded.map(upsertDay)) (trip.ts:229) with no batching and no error inspection. Server-side upsert_day rejects dayNumber>999 with HTTP 400 (days.py:72-73), but upsertDay’s {ok:false} is ignored, so days 1000..N silently fail while 1..999 persist. The local STATE keeps all N days, diverging from the server.

trips.title: Maps-unavailable country fallback creates the Day-0 anchor at lat/lng 0,0 (Gulf of Guinea)
trips.rootCause: The Maps-down fallback in _wirePlacePicker builds the picked place with lat:0,lng:0 (and viewport:null) because it only resolves a country code, not coordinates (_shared.ts:217-218). The create modal copies these straight onto the trip (trip.ts:184-185). The lazy Day-0 creator gates only on `typeof activeTrip.lat === 'number'` (handlers.ts:243-244) — and 0 IS a number — so it creates the anchor day with lat:0,lng:0 (handlers.ts:252-253). Nothing treats (0,0) as 'no pin yet' on the anchor path (todoMarkers.ts:181 special-cases 0,0 for markers, but the anchor day creation does not).

trips.title: Background /api/data poll replaces STATE.trips while the Edit-Trip modal is open, detaching the edited object
trips.rootCause: openEditTripModal captures a reference to a specific STATE.trips object (trip.ts:243). pullFromServer unconditionally does `STATE.trips = allTrips.filter(...)` (api.ts:183) — building brand-new objects — even when a modal is open; the modal-open guard at api.ts:335-336 only suppresses the re-NAVIGATE, not the STATE replacement. So mid-edit the modal’s `trip` becomes an orphan no longer in STATE. On submit, the modal mutates the detached object and emits state:changed (which re-renders from the fresh STATE objects, momentarily dropping the user’s edits), then upsertTrip(detached) sends the change with the updatedAt captured at modal-open. If anyone touched the trip server-side in between, that stamp is stale → 409 staleEdit toast (api.ts:413) for a single-editor user.

trips.title: 'Day deleted' success toast is a hardcoded English string (i18n gap)
trips.rootCause: deleteDay’s onConfirm calls showLiquidAlert('Day deleted') with a literal string instead of t(...) (home-mount/handlers.ts:321). Every other toast in this flow uses t(); this one was missed. The confirm dialog itself (errors.deleteDayTitle/Body/ConfirmBtn) is correctly localized, so only the success toast is wrong.

trips.title: Editing trip dates with all-undated existing days silently no-ops the date change
trips.rootCause: In the edit-dates branch, oldFirst/oldLast are parsed from numberedDays[0].date / [last].date (trip.ts:527-528). When those dates are empty, parseUTC('') returns null, so startChanged and endChanged (trip.ts:532-535) are both false (they require oldFirst/oldLast to be non-null). The rebase (line 539) and extend/shorten (line 565) blocks are gated on startChanged/endChanged, so nothing runs — the picked dates are written only to trip.dateFrom/dateTo (finalizeAndClose) but never propagated to the day rows. The 'numberedDays.length === 0' scaffold path (line 518) doesn’t apply because numbered days exist (just dateless).

itinerary.title: /api/sync bulk trip_days loop has NO authorization gate — cross-tenant day write/inject (IDOR sibling of the R2 /api/days fix)
itinerary.rootCause: The `/api/sync` trip_days loop (data.py:793-838) writes `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE` keyed only on `WHERE trip_days.deleted_at IS NULL`. It trusts `d['tripId']` verbatim and has NO membership check. The sibling loops in the SAME handler ARE gated: the trips loop does `if existing and t['id'] not in editable_trip_ids: continue` (data.py:302-306) and the expenses loop gates on `expense_writable_trip_ids`. The per-row POST /api/days route was hardened against exactly this (R2 fix, days.py:77-91, with test test_days_single_row_upsert_blocks_cross_trip_hijack), but the bulk-path sibling was missed. The loop also omits the dayNumber validation (negative/huge/fractional) that days.py:50-74 enforces. The first-party client now sends `{}` to /api/sync (api.ts:80), so there is no first-party trigger, but the route still accepts and writes the payload from any caller — and AI-accepted days use predictable ids `'day_'+Date.now()+'_'+idx` (useAiPlan.ts:466), making targeted overwrites feasible even without an id leak.

itinerary.title: Deleting a day silently RESURRECTS its pruned photos/documents — server delete_day media cascade undone by the client's loss-free union merge
itinerary.rootCause: Frontend deleteDay (handlers.ts:272-325) removes the day from STATE.tripDays and calls deleteDayOnServer, but never cleans the local `trip.photos`/`trip.documents`/`trip.markedPlaces` of items tagged with the deleted dayId, and never calls fetchTripMedia. delete_day bumps trips.media_updated_at when it prunes (days.py:338-347), so the client's next persistTripMedia gets a 409. The 409 handler (media.ts:128-159) merges via `_mergeMediaField`, which is a pure UNION by key (media.ts: keeps every server item + adds any LOCAL item missing from the server set). Because the server PRUNED the day-tagged items, they are 'missing' on the server side, so the union RE-ADDS the stale local copies and POSTs them back. The merge has zero deletion/tombstone awareness — the comment at media.ts:163-164 even claims 're-parking ... can't resurrect a peer's delete', which is exactly the wrong assumption: a server-side delete (cascade OR a peer removing a photo) is indistinguishable from 'an item the local side should re-add'.

itinerary.title: Parallel renumber upserts collide on UNIQUE(trip_id, day_number) after deleting a middle day → numbering gap + spurious 'another device updated this day' toast
itinerary.rootCause: deleteDay renumbers survivors in memory to a contiguous 1..N (handlers.ts:296-301) then persists them with `Promise.all(...map(upsertDay))` (handlers.ts:318-320) — UNORDERED parallel writes. The same pattern is in the edit-dates finalize (trip.ts:621). When numbers shift DOWNWARD into still-occupied slots, two parallel upserts race against the partial UNIQUE index idx_trip_days_trip_day_number(trip_id, day_number) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL. Example: survivors [d3→2, d4→3, d5→4]; if upsertDay(d5→4) executes before upsertDay(d4→3) commits, d5's target 4 still collides with d4's live row (number 4) → IntegrityError → days.py:161-181 returns 409. The client's 409 handler (api.ts:453-462) shows the stale-edit toast + pullFromServer but does NOT retry with a corrected number, so the survivor keeps its old number and a gap persists until reload.

itinerary.title: Read-only day modal (dayViewModal) is only half-internationalised — plan slot labels, notes labels and empty-states are hardcoded English
itinerary.rootCause: The dayView i18n block (en.ts:1571-1584) was added for this exact file ('Pre-fix every label ... was hardcoded English') but only covers the photos/documents/expert-tip section. The morning/afternoon/evening/notes/view-only/nothing-planned strings were left as literal English in the template (dayViewModal.ts), so t() is never called for them and they don't switch locale.

itinerary.title: Journaling modal is entirely hardcoded English (no i18n at all)
itinerary.rootCause: journalingModal.ts has no `import { t }` and uses raw English literals throughout the template literal and the save toast (lines 31-45). It was missed in the i18n migration that internationalised the sibling dayDetailModal.

itinerary.title: Day-pin flow alerts are hardcoded English
itinerary.rootCause: These three showLiquidAlert calls in handlers.ts pass raw English strings; no matching i18n keys exist (grep for the strings in locales/en.ts returns nothing). The surrounding code already imports t() and uses it for the delete-confirm and the Trip-Hub-cannot-delete error, so these were simply overlooked.

itinerary.title: Read-only day modal shows a blue 'Day 0' badge for the Trip Hub anchor instead of a Trip Hub chip
itinerary.rootCause: dayViewModal.ts only special-cases the anchor (Number(day.dayNumber)===0) for the photos/documents section labels (lines 104,110,113,118); the header badge unconditionally renders `Day ${day.dayNumber}` (line 68) and the title falls back to `Day ${dayNumber}` (line 72). The anchor-vs-numbered branching that dayDetailModal has (isAnchor, gold chip, Trip Hub title) was never ported to the read-only modal.

itinerary.title: Shortlist 'Add to AM/PM/Eve' ✓ marker and toggle use naive case-insensitive substring matching — false positives for short/substring place names corrupt the wrong plan line
itinerary.rootCause: Presence is detected with `planVals[time].includes(place.name.toLowerCase())` (line 744) and removal does `lines.findIndex(l => l.toLowerCase().includes(needle))` + splice (lines 862-863). Plain substring matching with no word-boundary or whole-line check; the add format is '- {name}' but the detector matches the bare name anywhere.

itinerary.title: Dropping a day pin on the map doesn't persist to local storage until 'Save pin' — navigating away loses the placed pin
itinerary.rootCause: The armed map-click listener in addDayPin sets day.lat/lon/lng and calls navigate('home', null, true) but does NOT emit('state:changed') (handlers.ts:113-119), so localStorage isn't updated and the server isn't written. Persistence happens only in saveDayPin (which emits + upsertDay). The in-memory mutation survives only until the next full reload/state hydration, after which it's gone. (cancelPinEdit and deleteDayPin both emit; the drop path is the inconsistent one.)

todo.title: Clean-slate / remove of a to-do place is silently reverted during the post-load 'cold' media window (deletes lose to the additive merge)
todo.rootCause: saveState() persists the whole STATE (incl. trip.markedPlaces) to localStorage, so on load loadState() shows the full to-do list before media is hydrated. But `_mediaLoadedTrips` is an in-memory Set that starts empty after every reload, so persistTripMedia() (media.ts:274) treats the trip as un-hydrated: it PARKS the write in `_pendingMedia` and calls fetchTripMedia. fetchTripMedia then does `_mergeMediaField(serverMedia.markedPlaces, pending.markedPlaces)` (media.ts:217) which is UNION-by-key — server items always survive, the parked snapshot only ADDS. A clean-slate parks `markedPlaces: []`, so the merge returns the full server array (every item resurrected) and flushes it back, also re-emitting STATE_CHANGED so the UI repaints the items the user just deleted. The guarding comment (media.ts:44 'During a cold window the only possible edit is an ADD — nothing is loaded yet to delete') is false: localStorage rehydration means items ARE visible and deletable before media loads.

todo.title: Two distinct marked places that share a name collide in the media merge — one is silently dropped (wrong dedup key)
todo.rootCause: _mergeMediaField dedups by `_mediaKey(item)` = `String(o.id ?? o.url ?? o.name ?? JSON.stringify(item))`. A MarkedPlace's canonical identity is `placeId` (the type's `id`/`url` fields are NEVER populated — neither toggleTodoListMembership (markedPlaces.ts:109) nor addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified (markedPlaces.ts:296) sets `id` or `url`). So `_mediaKey` falls through to `name`, and two different placeIds with the same name map to the same key — the second is treated as a duplicate and discarded. (Nameless items, e.g. an AI item with empty text/verifiedName, all collapse to key '' too.)

todo.title: Accept Plan orphans manually-pinned places' dayId — they silently disappear from every day's pane and per-day map markers
todo.rootCause: onAcceptPlan() deletes ALL existing numbered days (useAiPlan.ts:450-456) and creates brand-new ones with fresh ids `'day_'+Date.now()+'_'+idx` (useAiPlan.ts:466). dropAITaggedPlaces only removes source:'ai' entries, so a manual place survives — but it keeps its OLD dayId, which now matches no existing day. dayDetailModal._renderPlacesForSlot filters by `p.dayId === day.id` (dayDetailModal.ts:351) so it never matches; todoMarkers.ts:183-186 filters it out of every numbered-day view. There is no orphaned-dayId healing: state.ts only migrates documents/photos `dayId:null→anchorId` (state.ts:262-270), never validates markedPlaces.dayId against existing days.

todo.title: Legacy 🏖️ Sights icon is not healed for server-fetched media → a duplicate 'Sights' section + duplicate filter entry
todo.rootCause: The 🏖️→🏛️ heal only runs inside loadState() over localStorage data (state.ts:246). The authoritative markedPlaces for the active trip arrive LATER via fetchTripMedia(), which splices the server arrays straight into trip.markedPlaces (media.ts:204, 233) without re-running the heal. groupingIcon() normalises 📋→📍 but NOT 🏖️→🏛️ (todoCategories.ts:20), and 🏖️ is absent from CATEGORY_ORDER (todoCategories.ts:81), so a 🏖️ place forms its own trailing bucket. iconToLabel maps both 🏖️ and 🏛️ to 'Sights', producing two identically-labelled sections/filter rows.

todo.title: Day-Detail shortlist AM/PM/Eve buttons swap and de-localise their glyphs after the first interaction
todo.rootCause: Initial render uses the i18n strings shortlistBtnAm='☀️ AM', shortlistBtnPm='🌅 PM' (en.ts:1846) — sun=morning, matching _slotIcon at dayDetailModal.ts:304. But refreshShortlistButtons() rebuilds the label as a hardcoded literal `time==='morning' ? '🌅 AM' : time==='afternoon' ? '☀️ PM' : '🌙 Eve'` (dayDetailModal.ts:747) — sunset=morning (swapped) and not run through t(). refreshShortlistButtons runs on every input/toggle and on initial paint, overwriting the localised text.

todo.title: Day-Detail shortlist add/remove uses case-insensitive name substring matching — collides when one place name contains another
todo.rootCause: The shortlist toggle and its ✓ indicator detect presence by `textareaValue.toLowerCase().includes(place.name.toLowerCase())` (dayDetailModal.ts:744 for the ✓ marker, :852 for the add/remove decision) and remove the first line matching that substring (:862). A shorter name is a substring of the line written for a longer name, so the membership test and the line-removal both misfire across the two places.

todo.title: Hardcoded English strings across the to-do / marked-place surfaces (not localised)
todo.rootCause: These literals are written directly in JSX/HTML instead of through t(). Examples: Todo.tsx:643 'Plan with AI ✦', Todo.tsx:324 'View on Google Maps →', Todo.tsx:179/204 `Open ${p.name} on Google Maps`, Todo.tsx:241 'Hide details'/'Show details', todoMarkers.ts:157 'On your to-do list' pill + :145 'Day ${n}' chip + :151 'View on Google Maps →', TodoListPanel.tsx:373-374 Maps title/aria, dayDetailModal.ts:611 `${remaining} of ${items.length} left` (the initial render at :472 uses t('dayDetail.checklistRemaining') but the toggle handler overwrites it with the English literal).

ai.title: Accept Plan silently deletes ALL numbered days — wiping day journals, plan text, and permanently destroying day-attached photos/documents from disk
ai.rootCause: onAcceptPlan (useAiPlan.ts:450-456) does `existingNumbered = STATE.tripDays.filter(d => d.tripId === activeTrip.id && d.dayNumber > 0)` then `existingNumbered.forEach(d => deleteDayOnServer(d.id))` — it deletes EVERY numbered day regardless of origin, not just AI-created ones (there is no source/provenance check on days, unlike markedPlaces which has dropAITaggedPlaces). deleteDayOnServer hits DELETE /api/days/<id>, whose handler (days.py:264-352) runs a delete-cascade: it collects upload paths for trip-level photos_json/documents_json items with `dayId == day_id`, DROPS them from the JSON arrays, and calls delete_upload_files(upload_paths, owner_id) (days.py:351-352) which unlinks the files from disk. Photos are attached to days with the trip-day id as dayId (tripMediaModals.ts:429,782,953), and journal text is written to day.notes (journalingModal.ts:42). Because ItineraryOutput's `accepted` flag is component-local useState (ItineraryOutput.tsx:87) it resets on every remount, and the itinerary is re-shown from activeTrip.aiPlan on return to /ai — so the user can navigate away and back and re-accept, and a first-time AI user with auto-scaffolded days (modals/trip.ts:214, dayNumber starts at 1) loses everything on their FIRST accept.

ai.title: AI itinerary renders as EMPTY cards (and Accept writes '[object Object]') when the server has no Google Maps key — the documented 'graceful no-op' path is broken for the food/sights schema
ai.rootCause: When no Maps key is configured, _enrich_itinerary returns the itinerary UNCHANGED (integrations.py:307-308), so for the new food/sights schema the meal/sight objects keep their raw Gemini shape {name, why, fact} and never get a `text` field (text is only added by _enrich_one at integrations.py:339 on the enrichment path). The frontend renderer renderSlotItem keys ENTIRELY off `text`: `const text = String(v.text || ''); if (!text) return '';` (slots.ts:122-123) — so an object with `name` but no `text` renders nothing. MealBlock's has-content guard checks `p.text || p.name` (ItineraryOutput.tsx:291) so it thinks there IS content and calls renderRestaurantCard, which then emits an empty string. On Accept, itemToText (slots.ts:231-235) falls through to `String(item ?? '')` for an object lacking a string `text`, yielding '[object Object]', which flattenMealForTextarea writes into the day plan. The legacy items[] string schema is unaffected (strings render as bullets) — only the current food/sights schema is broken without a Maps key.

ai.title: A saved BYO Gemini key permanently disables the shared host-pool fallback — a user with an exhausted personal key is stuck with no way to generate
ai.rootCause: runGenerate always sends `gemini_key: (STATE.geminiApiKey || '').trim()` on every request (useAiPlan.ts:361). Server-side, if the key matches the shape regex, using_byo=true and `keys_to_try = [(0, user_key)]` ONLY (integrations.py:673-677) — the else-branch that builds the host pool is skipped. On a quota error from the BYO key, slot 0 is exempt from cooldown and the loop simply exhausts with no host-pool attempt (integrations.py:871-873). There is no 'fall back to host pool if BYO fails' path.

ai.title: Re-running Accept Plan orphans manually day-assigned places (dangling dayId) — they silently drop out of their day and fall into 'Unsorted'
ai.rootCause: Each Accept Plan run mints brand-new day ids (`'day_' + Date.now() + '_' + idx`, useAiPlan.ts:466) and deletes the old days. addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified only re-stamps the dayId of places that appear in the new plan (markedPlaces.ts:259). A manual place whose placeId isn't in the new plan retains its old dayId, which now references a deleted day. The day-detail and media-grouping code filter by `dayId === day.id` (dayDetailModal.ts:354) and bucket unmatched dayIds as '__orphan__'/Unsorted (tripMediaModals.ts:97), so the assignment is effectively lost.

ai.title: numDays has no client-side upper bound — request a 31+ day range and the result heading lies ('60-Day Itinerary' but only 30 days render/accept)
ai.rootCause: Client computes numDays = Math.max(1, Math.round(diff)+1) with NO upper clamp (useAiPlan.ts:291-294) and stores it as activeTrip.aiNumDays (line 328). The heading renders savedNumDays = activeTrip.aiNumDays (AI.tsx:78 → ItineraryOutput.tsx:116, key ai.resultHeading '{numDays}-Day {country} Itinerary'). The server clamps num_days = max(1, min(30, ...)) (integrations.py:602), so the actual itinerary length is capped at 30 while the heading shows the uncapped client value.

ai.title: Hardcoded English strings on the AI surface (BYO key status, card aria-labels, 'unverified' chip + tooltip) break i18n for es/fr/pt users
ai.rootCause: These strings are inline literals instead of t() lookups. Notably useAiPlan.ts keyStatus uses t('ai.keyStatusEmpty') for the empty case (line 218) but hardcodes the 'saved' and 'format off' branches (lines 221-225). slots.ts:180/209 build raw HTML with English aria-label/title/chip text; AIUsageCard.tsx:96 and TodoListPanel.tsx:373-374 hardcode aria-label/title.

maps.title: POI 'always anchor' categories (Medical/Pets/Schools/Sports/Transit/Traffic) ignore useAnchorAlways at runtime — pins follow the day pin and Settings lies
maps.rootCause: shouldForceAnchor() reads ONLY STATE.preferences?.poiAnchoring?.[cat.key] === 'anchor' and never falls back to cat.useAnchorAlways. The documented contract (state.ts:94-99 and types.d.ts:634-636: 'Empty / missing keys fall back to the category's useAnchorAlways flag') was lost in the React migration — the comment still points at the old pages/home.js. Because the default is {}, shouldForceAnchor returns false for all six categories, so (a) resolveSearchCenter uses the selected day instead of the anchor, and (b) the onSelectedDayChange skip-guard at HeroMap.tsx:624 (`if (cat && shouldForceAnchor(cat)) return;`) never triggers, so they needlessly re-fetch on day change. Settings.tsx:596-601 computes effectiveAnchor WITH the useAnchorAlways fallback, so the UI and the runtime disagree.

maps.title: Adding/removing a place to the to-do list from a map InfoWindow does not repaint to-do markers — map diverges from saved state until remount
maps.rootCause: The InfoWindow toggle handler (wireInfoWindowMarkButtons.todoBtn.onclick, HeroMap.tsx:320-331) calls toggleTodoListMembership + emit('state:changed') + upsertTrip + refresh() but never calls repaintTodoMarkers(). HeroMap's map-setup useEffect depends only on [mapRetryTick], so emit('state:changed') re-renders React but does NOT re-run the effect, so no marker repaint happens. repaintTodoMarkers() is only invoked on initial mount (line 662) and inside onSelectedDayChange (line 620).

maps.title: Map InfoWindows + search dropdown are hardcoded English — every place bubble and the search UI ignore the active locale
maps.rootCause: These strings are written as literal English in the imperative innerHTML builders (HeroMap.tsx buildInfoWindowHtml, mapSearch.ts renderPredictions/_FALLBACK_CAT.label, todoMarkers.ts openTodoInfoWindow, dayMarkers.ts openDayPinInfoWindow) and in MapSearchBar.tsx's static JSX, rather than going through t(). The surrounding surface (e.g. home.searchMapPlaceholder, poiTooltips.*) IS localized, confirming this is an oversight not a deliberate degraded path.

maps.title: Map search dropdown has no combobox/listbox semantics and no keyboard navigation — unusable for keyboard/screen-reader users
maps.rootCause: MapSearchBar.tsx renders a plain <input> (no role=combobox, aria-expanded, aria-controls, aria-autocomplete) and an empty results <div> (no role=listbox). mapSearch.ts renderPredictions emits <button class=map-search-row> rows with no role=option, no aria-selected, no id/aria-activedescendant wiring, and there is no keydown handler anywhere (grep for keydown/ArrowDown/Enter returns nothing). Selection is click-only.

maps.title: Selecting a search result that fails getDetails is a silent dead-end — no marker, no error, dropdown already closed
maps.rootCause: fetchDetails (mapSearch.ts:175-182) calls svc.getDetails and, on any status !== OK or null place, just `return`s with no user feedback. The click handler (line 224-232) has already called hideResults() and set the input value before fetchDetails runs, so there's no remaining UI cue. Contrast with renderPredictions which at least shows 'No matches.' for ZERO_RESULTS.

maps.title: Autocomplete predictions can render out of order (stale response overwrites newer) — no request-sequence guard
maps.rootCause: The 220ms debounce (typingTimer) limits request frequency but does not serialize responses. getPlacePredictions callbacks (line 210) call renderPredictions(preds) with no token/sequence check, so a slower earlier response that lands after a faster later one wins. There is no 'latest request id' comparison and no comparison of the response against the current searchInput.value.

maps.title: Places result on the to-do list double-pins on the map — POI/search pin and to-do pin overlap because skipPlaceIds is never passed
maps.rootCause: paintTodoMarkers supports a skipPlaceIds Set (documented at todoMarkers.ts:44-46, applied at line 188-189 'so the map doesn't double-pin'), but the only caller, repaintTodoMarkers in HeroMap.tsx:654-660, never passes skipPlaceIds. The set of currently-painted POI/search place_ids (available from placesMarkers / searchMarker) is never collected and forwarded.

maps.title: Map search bar offers an interactive search box that silently does nothing when the Maps API key is missing/blocked
maps.rootCause: If GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is unset, index.html:134 loads the Maps JS with an empty key, so google.maps never initializes and whenGoogleMapsReady() rejects. wireMapSearchBanner early-returns at mapSearch.ts:67 (no AutocompleteService), so NONE of the input/clear/results listeners are attached, but MapSearchBar.tsx unconditionally renders the input strip. There is no disabled/placeholder-changed/hidden state for the Maps-down case.

maps.title: wireMapSearchBanner leaks a document-level click listener on every HeroMap mount (never removed in cleanup)
maps.rootCause: mapSearch.ts:246 attaches document.addEventListener('click', …) but wireMapSearchBanner returns void and provides no teardown. HeroMap's effect cleanup (HeroMap.tsx:852-865) only removes the poiTogglesEl listener; it has no reference to remove the mapSearch document listener. The input/results/clear listeners are on React-owned nodes that get GC'd, but the document-level one persists.

maps.title: Map search + POI use deprecated legacy Places API (AutocompleteService / PlacesService) — future breakage risk
maps.rootCause: mapSearch.ts uses new google.maps.places.AutocompleteService() + svc.getDetails; HeroMap.tsx uses new google.maps.places.PlacesService(map) + svc.nearbySearch with RankBy.DISTANCE / radius. No migration to the newer AutocompleteSuggestion / Place (importLibrary('places')) classes exists.

companions.title: Friend-invite strips the new companion's linkedUserId server-side (upsert fires before the member row exists) → 'Pending' badge silently reverts to 'Unlinked' and a duplicate chip appears when they accept
companions.rootCause: In the friend-add handler the writes run in the order: emit → `void upsertTrip(trip)` (fires POST /api/trips immediately, carrying the companion with the freshly-set `linkedUserId`) → `await inviteTripMember(...)` (creates the `pending` trip_members row). The server's `_cleaned_companions` (trips.py:42-62) builds `verified_linked_ids` = the set of user_ids currently in `trip_members`, and `clean_companions` (validators.py:447-449) coerces any `linkedUserId` NOT in that set to None. Because the upsert reaches SQLite before the invite has created the member row, `friendId` is not yet a member, so the link is stripped and the companion is persisted as name-only. The local STATE keeps the link optimistically, but `pullFromServer` overwrites `trip.companions` wholesale from the server value (`companions: normalizeTripCompanions(t.companions)` at api.ts:149; only the 4 heavy media fields are merged, never companions), so the next 15s poll clobbers the optimistic link with the stripped server copy. Nothing re-links companions from trip_members on read, so once the friend accepts, `buildRosterChips` can't dedupe the companion against the member (the `linkedUserId` is gone) and renders both.

companions.title: Invite failures (409 role-conflict, 404 blocked/unknown user, 403) are swallowed: companion is still linked + persisted and a false 'invited' success toast shows
companions.rootCause: `inviteTripMember` uses `_post` (api/core.ts:296), which returns the raw Response (or undefined on network error) and never throws. The handler does `await inviteTripMember(trip.id, friendId, role)` (companions.ts:369) but ignores the result entirely, then unconditionally sets the link (lines 353-365), calls upsertTrip, and fires the success toast (line 372). The server returns 409 'Member already accepted with a different role' (trips.py:899-903) and 404 for blocked/nonexistent targets (trips.py:869-876), none of which surface to the user.

companions.title: Budgeteers are mislabelled as 'Relaxer' on the trip header role badge
companions.rootCause: The header renders the badge with `{!tripIsEditable ? <Relaxer badge> : null}` where `tripIsEditable = canEdit(activeTrip)` (TripBody.tsx:138), and `canEdit` is planner-only (permissions.ts:49-51). So for BOTH relaxers and budgeteers `tripIsEditable` is false, and the same hardcoded '👁 Relaxer' badge (companions.relaxerBadgeTitle / roleRelaxer) is shown. There is no branch for the budgeteer role.

companions.title: Linking a friend to a same-named existing unlinked companion silently merges two different people
companions.rootCause: In the brand-new-add branch, `findTripCompanion(trip, friendName)` (companions.ts:360) matches purely by name, case-insensitively (companions.ts:45-57), and if an unlinked row exists it sets `existingByName.linkedUserId = friendId` (line 362). There is no check that the existing unlinked entry actually refers to the same person. Because `addTripCompanion` also dedupes by name (companions.ts:144-150), the roster can never hold two 'Alex' rows, forcing the merge.

companions.title: Companion remove-lock is case-sensitive while the rest of the companion system is case-insensitive — a referenced companion can lose its 🔒 and be removed
companions.rootCause: `referencedNames` is built from raw `e.who` + split keys (companions.ts:69-74) and the lock test is `referencedNames.has(c.name)` (line 93) — an EXACT, case-sensitive Set match. Everywhere else the app treats companion names case-insensitively (findTripCompanion at companions.ts:45-57, and clean_companions case-folds server-side). So 'alice' in an expense vs an 'Alice' companion (e.g. a name typed with different casing into the ManualTab free-text 'who' field, or a CSV upload) won't match the lock, dropping the protection. (Balance math still uses a ghost-roster union so money isn't lost, but the lock — the user-facing guard — is bypassed.)

companions.title: Non-owner Planner can invite and kick other members via the API even though the UI presents roster management as owner-only
companions.rootCause: Server invite gate is `can_edit_trip` (= planner, trips.py:856) and remove gate is `can_edit_trip` for non-self removals (trips.py:1099), only blocking removal of the owner (trips.py:1101). The frontend permission helpers gate the same actions on `isOwner` (permissions.ts:64-79). The two layers disagree on who controls the roster.

expenses.title: Batch import never persists auto-created categories (syncWithServer is an empty no-op) — imported expenses lose their category on reload
expenses.rootCause: runBatchImport() pushes newly-inferred categories into STATE.categories (upload.ts:417-419) and at the end calls `void syncWithServer()` (line 463) whose comment claims it 'persists the categories the import created'. But syncWithServer() was gutted: its body is now `JSON.stringify({})` — a connectivity probe that sends NOTHING (api.ts:74-81). Categories now persist ONLY via the per-row delta endpoint syncCategories() (/api/categories), which upload.ts never imports or calls. The per-row upsertExpense writes the expense with a category_id pointing at the new local category, and the server stores that category_id as a free string with no existence check (routes/expenses.py has no FROM categories validation). On the next /api/data pull, STATE.categories is overwritten with the server's set (api.ts:236), which lacks the import-created category, so e.categoryId is now dangling and HistoryTab renders `cat ? cat.icon : '💰'` (HistoryTab.tsx:297,311).

expenses.title: Batch import never persists the companions it auto-adds — imported payers/splitters vanish on reload and become unselectable in the manual form
expenses.rootCause: runBatchImport() calls addTripCompanion(activeTrip, who) and addTripCompanion for each split name (upload.ts:390-398), which mutates activeTrip.companions in STATE. Companions persist only as part of a trip upsert (upsertTrip → /api/trips), but the import never calls upsertTrip, and the only other persistence channel, syncWithServer(), now sends an empty body (api.ts:74-81). So the roster mutation lives in localStorage/STATE only; the next /api/data pull overwrites STATE.trips with server truth (no imported companions). HistoryTab masks this with a fallback that derives payers from expense `who` values (HistoryTab.tsx:64-74), but ManualTab's 'Who paid' select has NO such fallback (ManualTab.tsx:531-540) — it shows only activeTrip.companions, so after reload the imported payers are unselectable.

expenses.title: Batch import reports 'Imported N' optimistically; rows the server rejects (amount > 1e9, dup cross-trip id, archived, stale) are silently dropped
expenses.rootCause: runBatchImport increments `added` and pushes to STATE.expenses purely on client-side optimistic success, then fires `void upsertExpense(expense)` (upload.ts:446) WITHOUT awaiting or inspecting the result. The client filter only rejects non-finite/<=0/no-rate values (line 374); it has NO upper bound, while the server's validate_money caps at _MAX_MONEY = 1e9 (validators.py:162-165) and the route returns 400. Because the POST result is discarded, a 400/403/409 is invisible: the row is counted, shown, and only vanishes when /api/data next overwrites STATE.expenses. (Contrast the manual form, which DOES await upsertExpense and shows 'saveFailed' — upload does not.)

expenses.title: Popular-format default split is computed against a roster that grows row-by-row, so equal-split percentages depend on import order
expenses.rootCause: Inside the per-row loop, addTripCompanion(activeTrip, who) runs first (upload.ts:390-392), then the no-splits default does `const tripRoster = getTripCompanionNames(activeTrip)` and divides 100/tripRoster.length across the CURRENT roster (lines 404-408). Because the roster accumulates as rows are processed, row 1 (only Alice seen) → {Alice:100}; row 2 (Bob now seen) → {Alice:50,Bob:50}; etc. For the canonical 'I have a Tricount, let me import the whole trip' flow the roster starts empty, so early rows are systematically mis-split.

expenses.title: Splitwise import hardcodes payer 'Me' and creates a literal 'Me' companion, mislabeling every imported row's payer
expenses.rootCause: The splitwise branch sets `who = 'Me'` unconditionally (upload.ts:341) because the chosen Splitwise column layout [Date, Description, Category, Cost, Currency] has no payer column. addTripCompanion(activeTrip, 'Me') then plants 'Me' in the roster (line 390-392). Splitwise's real CSV export does contain per-person paid/owed columns, but this mapping ignores them, so the payer attribution is fabricated and balances are wrong whenever the user wasn't the sole payer.

expenses.title: No row-count cap or concurrency control on batch import — a large file fires thousands of parallel POSTs and janks the tab
expenses.rootCause: runBatchImport processes parsedRows in a synchronous forEach with no size cap (upload.ts:314), building thousands of Expense objects + mutating STATE + calling addTripCompanion per row, then firing `void upsertExpense(expense)` for every row (line 446). upsertExpense → apiFetch uses raw fetch with no queue/throttle (api/core.ts:201-235), so N rows = N concurrent POSTs launched in one tick. BatchUpload's parse also has no row cap (BatchUpload.tsx:95-97). There is no batching, chunking, or max-rows guard anywhere on this path.

expenses.title: Batch import preview filters rows on the FIRST cell being truthy, silently dropping valid rows with an empty first column
expenses.rootCause: The parser keeps a row only if `r.length > 0 && r[0]` (BatchUpload.tsx:97) — i.e. it requires the FIRST cell to be truthy. But which column is meaningful depends on the format/mapping: Tricount keys on column A (Title) but the amount/payer are elsewhere; a custom format may map its required fields to columns B+. A row with a blank first cell but real data in the mapped columns is discarded before runBatchImport's own per-row validation/skip-reporting runs, so it's invisibly lost rather than reported.

expenses.title: Import date parser silently discards any date with a 2-digit year, importing common CSV dates as undated 'Global'
expenses.rootCause: parseCellDate requires a 4-digit year token: `const yIdx = parts.findIndex(p => /^\d{4}$/.test(p)); if (yIdx === -1) return '';` (upload.ts:231-232). A 2-digit year never matches, so the function returns '' and the expense is stored with date=''. There's no attempt to expand a 2-digit year (e.g. 20YY) and no per-row signal that the date was dropped, so it looks like the dates were simply absent.

expenses.title: History value-range filter is labeled '(€)' and filters on stored EUR euroValue, but the row's converted line shows the user's (non-EUR) home currency
expenses.rootCause: applyHistoryFilters compares against e.euroValue (the frozen EUR value) for minVal/maxVal (helpers.ts:134-137), and the filter label is hardcoded '(€)' (HistoryTab.tsx:255). But the row's converted helper line uses formatHome(e.value, e.currency), i.e. the user's home currency via live conversion (HistoryTab.tsx:302-303). So the number the user sees and the number the filter ranges over are in different currencies for any non-EUR home.

settlements.title: No-rate-currency settle silently fabricates a 1:1 euroValue — corrupts EUR/Global/Insights balances (or hard-blocks settling a real exotic-currency debt)
settlements.rootCause: settleDebt (actions.ts:115) unconditionally computes `euroValue = convertCurrency(amount, currency, 'EUR')`. For a no-rate currency, convertCurrency → _rateFor (currency.ts:71-78) falls through to `CONVERSION_RATES[upper] || 1` = 1 on BOTH sides, so it returns the raw foreign amount unchanged (50000 ARS → €50000). It then always spreads that fabricated euroValue into the POST. Server create_settlement only rejects a no-rate non-EUR settlement when `rate is None AND euro_value is None` (settlements.py:222-228); because the client always sends a (wrong) euroValue, that gate never fires, and compute_euro_value's cold path (fx_rates.py:228) just returns the client hint verbatim. The expense form has the correct pattern — `needsManualEuro = !!selCurrency && !hasRate(selCurrency)` (ManualTab.tsx:108) forces an explicit EUR amount for no-rate currencies — but the settlement modal and the one-click Settle button have NO such gate. The suggested-debt Settle button (SettlementView.tsx:310-320) renders for every currency gated only on tripIsEditable; `hasRate(d.currency)` at line 302 controls only the cosmetic '≈ EUR' hint, not the button.

settlements.title: Settlement History groups rows by UTC calendar date — evening settlements show under the wrong day for non-UTC users, and same-day rows have unstable ordering
settlements.rootCause: collectSettlementHistory (viewData.ts:178) derives the grouping/sort key as `date: (s.createdAt || '').slice(0,10)`. createdAt comes from settlements.created_at, which is SQLite `DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` = UTC (baseline.py:45). Slicing the first 10 chars yields the UTC calendar date, not the viewer's local date. The History tab then compares that key against `new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)` (also UTC) for Today/Yesterday labels (SettlementView.tsx:370-373) and sorts by `new Date(b.date) - new Date(a.date)` (viewData.ts:184), which—because the time component was dropped—cannot order same-day rows.

settlements.title: Hardcoded English 'Couldn't undo' toast on settlement-delete failure (i18n gap)
settlements.rootCause: deleteSettlement's failure branch builds the message as a raw template literal: `Couldn't undo: ${result.error || 'Network error'}` (actions.ts:261) instead of going through t(). The codebase already has the localized sibling `settlement.toastSettlementFailed: 'Settlement failed: {error}'` (en.ts:651, mirrored across locales) used on the create path — the delete path simply wasn't wired to an equivalent key. Because it's a hardcoded literal it bypasses the compile-time Translations check entirely.

budgets.title: Budget card "was X" badge converts the original amount to home currency, destroying the foreign figure it exists to show (no-rate currencies show the raw number with the wrong symbol)
budgets.rootCause: Budgets.tsx:354 renders the original amount with `formatHome(b.originalAmount || 0, b.originalCurrency)`. `formatHome(amount, from)` converts `from`→the user's HOME currency and formats with the HOME symbol (currency.ts:137-141), discarding `b.originalCurrency` entirely. The badge's whole purpose (per the `originalAmount`/`originalCurrency` columns and budgets.py:89-96) is to preserve the user-typed foreign amount. The badge only renders when `b.originalCurrency !== 'EUR'` (line 353), so it is ALWAYS wrong when shown. For no-rate currencies `convertCurrency` falls back to rate=1 (currency.ts:71-78), so the foreign number passes through unscaled but gets the home symbol. The server-side PDF renderer gets this right — it uses `tr.money(orig_curr, orig_amount)` (pdf/__init__.py:696-697) — proving the intended behavior; the web card diverged.

budgets.title: Create-budget modal shows "Budget saved" and keeps the optimistic row even when the server REJECTS the write (409 duplicate-scope / 4xx) — row then silently vanishes on next sync
budgets.rootCause: helpers.ts:408-411 does `await upsertBudget(budget); close(); showLiquidAlert(savedToast)` inside a try/catch. But `upsertBudget` → `_upsertWithUpdatedAt` (api.ts:376) RETURNS a resolved `{ok:false,status:409|4xx}` on any non-2xx (it only throws nothing — even the network catch at api.ts:433 returns `{ok:false}`). So the catch block (helpers.ts:412-417) is dead for server rejections; the success path always runs. The modal never inspects `result.ok`. This is the FE-2 "honest save" pattern that WAS applied to the expense form (ManualTab.tsx:473-474 checks `if (!res || !res.ok)`) but missed for budgets. On 409 the api layer also shows `errors.staleEdit` + calls `pullFromServer()` (api.ts:413-416), producing a double-toast and the vanishing-row desync.

budgets.title: Account-wide ('all trips') budget is silently hidden and unrecoverable when the active trip is the only trip carrying budgets
budgets.rootCause: On mount the filter lazy-initializes to the active trip when that trip has any budget: `filterTrip = activeTripId && allBudgets.some(b => b.tripId === activeTripId) ? activeTripId : ''` (Budgets.tsx:46-50). `visibleBudgets` then filters to an EXACT tripId match (line 52-53), excluding tripId='all' budgets. The filter chips (including the "All trips" reset chip) only render when `tripsInBudgets.length > 1` (line 83), and `tripsInBudgets` excludes 'all' (line 80-81). So with budgets = [{tripId:'t1'},{tripId:'all'}] and active=t1: filterTrip='t1', showTripChips=false → the 'all' budget is filtered out with no UI to recover. `activeTripId` defaults to `STATE.trips[0].id` on every load (api.ts:201), so this state is common, not exotic.

budgets.title: Overall card 'over-budget' threshold uses strict `>` while individual cards use `>=` — same numbers read "Over budget" on the card but "Near limit" on the Overall
budgets.rootCause: The per-card `budgetStatus` was tightened (audit B5) to treat hitting the ceiling as over: `if (target > 0 && spent >= target) tier='over'` (helpers.ts:200). The Overall tier was left at strict greater-than: `totalSpent > totalAllocated ? 'over' : overallPct > 80 ? 'near' : 'ok'` (Budgets.tsx:72-76). At exact equality (pct=100) the Overall falls through to the `>80` 'near' branch. The B5 fix didn't propagate to the Overall card.

budgets.title: Read side hard-assumes budget.amount is EUR and ignores the stored currency column — a non-EUR budget from the API/CSV-import/legacy path is misread as euros
budgets.rootCause: The EUR-canonical invariant for budget.amount is enforced ONLY by the create modal (helpers.ts:395-401 converts to EUR and forces `currency:'EUR'`). The server (budgets.py:48-49) stores `amount` and `currency` verbatim with NO server-side conversion to EUR, and the read serializer ships both (data.py:1205-1222). But the frontend NEVER reads `b.currency` anywhere — `allocatedAcrossBudgets`, `budgetStatus`, and both card renders all pass `b.amount` to `formatHome(..., 'EUR')`. So any write path other than the modal (the raw `/api/budgets` API, a future CSV import, or legacy data — all explicitly acknowledged as live paths in budgets.py:62-63) can desynchronize amount/currency from the EUR assumption and be misread.

budgets.title: Budgets page recomputes spend O(budgets × expenses) three times per render with no memoization
budgets.rootCause: None of the aggregations are memoized. Per render the component computes `allocatedAcrossBudgets(visibleBudgets)` (O(budgets²) containment), `spentAcrossBudgets(visibleBudgets)` (O(expenses × budgets)), `overBudgetCount` which calls `budgetStatus(b)` (= `spentForBudget`, O(expenses)) for every visible budget (Budgets.tsx:67), and then the card map calls `budgetStatus(b)` AGAIN per card (line 301) — a second full O(budgets × expenses) pass. The component subscribes to `expenses` (line 34), so every expense add/edit re-runs all of it. ~3× O(budgets × expenses) + O(budgets²) per render.

insights.title: Insights 'Worth today' silently wrong for ~30 live-only currencies (SEK/NOK/DKK/THB/TRY/PLN…) because Model-A vs Model-B hinges on a non-reactive, fire-and-forget FX-overlay race
insights.rootCause: The Model-A (has-FX) vs Model-B (no-FX) branch is decided by hasRate(curUp) (presentValue.ts:240). hasRate() only returns true for EUR + CONVERSION_RATES (17 codes) + the live overlay _liveRates (currency.ts:88-93). The live overlay is populated by refreshFxRates() which is fired fire-and-forget at boot (main.ts:106, `void refreshFxRates()`) and, on success, calls setLiveFxRates() which mutates a MODULE-PRIVATE object and does NOT emit STATE_CHANGED (currency.ts:62-69). The Insights useMemo depends on rateCache/cpiCache/etc. but never on the live overlay, so (a) before the overlay lands hasRate('SEK')=false → Model B is used, and (b) even after it lands the memo does not recompute, and (c) if /api/fx-rates fails outright the wrong model persists for the whole session. The hero gate (heroCalculating, Insights.tsx:563) only waits on the CPI fetch, never on the live overlay, so the wrong figure is shown as final. The historical 'Spent' leg uses rateCache (Frankfurter, which DOES carry SEK), so only the Today leg is wrong → leg mismatch.

insights.title: 'Add currency' control in the rates editor is mislabeled: button reuses the 'Add year' i18n key
insights.rootCause: RatesEditor.tsx:786 uses {t('settings.ratesAddYear')} for the add-currency button inside the add-currency group; the dedicated label key is ratesAddCurrencyLabel and there is no shared neutral 'Add' action key intended for both. The add-year button at line 815 correctly uses ratesAddYear.

insights.title: Auto-fill FX suggestion for a PAST year on a non-EUR home mixes at-the-time EUR cost with today's EUR→home rate
insights.rootCause: computeAutoRate() mode 'fx' computes homeValue = convertFn(euro,'EUR',homeCur) where euro is the at-the-time euroValue but convertFn only knows today's rate (manualRates.ts:186-188). implied = homeValue/value then becomes a year-rate suggestion. There is no historical EUR→home factor applied, so for home≠EUR the implied rate blends two epochs.

insights.title: Top-spender amount renders bare '0' (no currency symbol) when total display spend is zero
insights.rootCause: Insights.tsx:1245 ternary `{totalDisplay > 0 ? targetSym + formatNumberForCurrency(topSpenderAmount, targetCurr) : '0'}` emits the literal string '0' instead of the formatted/symboled value in the zero case.

rates-editor.title: EU-locale CSV import (semicolon delimiter + decimal comma) silently shatters every rate into garbage
rates-editor.rootCause: RatesEditor.parseCsv splits each line on the regex /[,;\t]/ (line 478). For a EU-locale cell like `0,88` the comma is the DECIMAL separator, but the split treats it as a field delimiter, so `USD;0,88;0,90` becomes ['USD','0','88','0','90']. parseRatesGrid then maps the year columns onto the shards: USD/2020 = 88, GBP/2021 = 1, GBP/2020 = 17 (empirically confirmed via probe). The locale-aware decimal-comma logic in manualRates.parseRateAmount is defeated because parseCsv has already torn the comma out before parseRatesGrid sees the cell. The .xlsx path (SheetJS typed cells) is immune, but the .csv path — which is exactly what 'Download template' produces and what most users round-trip — is broken for every comma-decimal locale (pt/es/fr/de…).

rates-editor.title: Auto-fill and template pin a bogus 1:1 FX (1 unit = 1 EUR) for no-live-rate currencies (ARS/EGP/VND/TRY…)
rates-editor.rootCause: computeAutoRate's FX fallback is `convertFn(1, cur, home)` (manualRates.ts:191) and the placeholder uses `convertCurrency(1, cur, home)` (RatesEditor.tsx:213). convertCurrency silently returns the input unchanged for unknown codes (_rateFor → `CONVERSION_RATES[upper] || 1`, currency.ts:77), so the result is 1, which passes the `rate > 0` / `Number.isFinite && r>0` guards. Neither the editor, computeAutoRate, nor the placeholder consults hasRate(), unlike presentValue.ts which carefully routes no-rate currencies to Model B. Confirmed via probe: computeAutoRate('fx','ARS',currentYear,[],undefined,'EUR',convertFn)===1 (and also ===1 for 2015).

rates-editor.title: Download template bakes auto values as if user-entered — incl. past-year FX = today's rate — so a round-trip silently freezes wrong overrides
rates-editor.rootCause: currentValueForTemplate (RatesEditor.tsx:447) calls computeAutoRate for any blank cell with NO per-year guard. The auto-fill button deliberately skips past-year FX cells that have no expenses (RatesEditor.tsx:413-419, the PV4-2 guard: today's rate is a misleading baseline for an old year), but the template path reintroduces exactly that value. On re-import every populated cell is written to draft and Save persists it as a hard override (writeMerged), converting auto cells into permanent pins.

rates-editor.title: Deleting a year column or currency row clears the dirty flag, stranding other unsaved edits (Save disabled, silent loss on navigate)
rates-editor.rootCause: removeYear (line 354) and removeCurrency (line 374) unconditionally call setDirty(false) after persisting only the deleted row/column. They don't account for other cells the user has edited in the draft. The Save button is gated on `disabled={!dirty}` (line 827), so clearing dirty disables it while unsaved edits remain in `draft`.

rates-editor.title: Switching the FX↔Inflation Personalization tab discards unsaved rate edits with no confirmation
rates-editor.rootCause: Personalization renders the editor conditionally per tab (`{tab === 'fx' ? <RatesEditor mode="fx"/> : null}`), so switching tabs unmounts the component and its local `draft` state. Edits live only in component state until Save (by design, RatesEditor.tsx:24-25), and nothing blocks/preserves them across an unmount.

rates-editor.title: Importing a well-formed FX file that only contains the home-currency row shows a false 'couldn't read that file' error
rates-editor.rootCause: applyParsedCells skips every cell where `isFx && currency === home` (lines 488, 498) since FX has no home row, so `applied` stays 0. onImportFile treats `applied === 0` as a hard failure and calls setImportError(true) (lines 532-534), conflating 'nothing applicable' with 'unreadable file'.

templates.title: Template-imported AI to-do places (source:'ai') are silently DELETED on the new owner's first Accept Plan
templates.rootCause: This is a direct sibling of the just-fixed Accept-Plan/markedPlaces data-loss bug. A creator who builds an itinerary with the AI planner gets markedPlaces stamped `source:'ai'` AND `forManual:true` (markedPlaces.ts:314-320 in addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified) — so they show in the to-do list. _build_template_snapshot/_clean_marked_places (templates.py:85-109,182-186) copy every field EXCEPT dayId/timeOfDay, so `source:'ai'` is preserved verbatim into the snapshot. _instantiate_template (templates.py:220,251) re-runs _clean_marked_places and writes the places straight into the new trip's marked_places_json with `source:'ai'` intact. The new owner has no idea these are 'ai'-tagged. On their first Accept Plan, useAiPlan.ts:459 calls dropAITaggedPlaces(activeTrip) which filters out EVERY `source==='ai'` place (markedPlaces.ts:345-348) — deleting the imported template content. The recent fix only stopped addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified from PROMOTING a manual place to 'ai'; it did nothing for places that arrive already stamped 'ai' from a template (or clone — _clone_trip_record copies marked_places_json verbatim too, trips.py:1307-1309).

templates.title: Instantiated template (and clone) day rows keep NULL dates that never populate when the new owner sets a trip date range
templates.rootCause: _instantiate_template inserts every day with date=NULL (templates.py:285,290) — by design, matching _clone_trip_record which NULLs dates and whose comment (trips.py:1395-1406) explicitly states the frontend is supposed to populate fresh dates when the new owner picks a start. But the Edit-Trip date logic can't do that: it pre-fills the picker from numberedDays[0].date (trip.ts:319) which is '' for an instantiated trip, and its rebase/extend branches are gated on `startChanged = newStart && oldFirst && ...` / `endChanged = newEnd && oldLast && ...` (trip.ts:532-535). oldFirst/oldLast come from parseUTC(numberedDays[0|N].date || '') which is null when the days have no dates, so both guards short-circuit to false and NO date is ever written to the day rows (the only path that scaffolds fresh dates is the numberedDays.length===0 branch, which doesn't apply here because the days exist). The documented clone/template date-assignment contract is therefore broken for both clone and template instantiation.

templates.title: Public template preview counts the internal Trip-Hub anchor as a day (off-by-one 'days planned') and renders it as a ★ row to strangers
templates.rootCause: _build_template_snapshot selects ALL non-deleted trip_days ORDER BY day_number with no `day_number > 0` filter (templates.py:162-167), so the snapshot's `days` array includes the lazily-created Trip-Hub anchor (dayNumber 0; created client-side in pages/home-mount/handlers.ts:246-260, so it exists for any trip the creator has opened on Home). fetch_template_preview then sets dayCount = len(days) (templates.py:527), counting day 0, and passes day 0 through to template.html, which renders dayNumber==0 as a ★ 'Trip Hub' row (template.html:106-111). The anchor usually has empty plan slots so it shows as a contentless extra row.

sharing.title: Clone-from-share copies full day-plan text, silently bypassing the owner's "Show day-by-day plans" privacy toggle
sharing.rootCause: fetch_share_payload (public.py:591-621) gates plan SELECT/render on share_show_plans, but the clone path does NOT. clone_trip_from_share_token (trips.py:1519) calls the shared _clone_trip_record, which unconditionally SELECTs morning/afternoon/evening/tip (trips.py:1384-1389) and INSERTs them into the new trip's days (trips.py:1407-1425). The function takes no plan-gating parameter and never reads share_show_plans. The Share modal markets the toggle as a privacy control (en.ts togglePlansBody: 'Morning / afternoon / evening notes per day. Photos and documents stay private.'), so the bypass directly violates the stated contract.

sharing.title: Cloned to-do places keep dangling dayId/timeOfDay pointing at the source trip's days — invisible on every per-day map view (sibling of the just-fixed template bug)
sharing.rootCause: _clone_trip_record copies marked_places_json VERBATIM (trips.py:1307-1309) while generating brand-new day ids for the cloned days (trips.py:1393, 1414). MarkedPlace.dayId (and AI timeOfDay) therefore reference the SOURCE trip's day ids, which do not exist in the clone. todoMarkers.ts:183 hides any place whose dayId != selectedDayId when on a numbered day, and the day chip lookup days.find(d => d.id === place.dayId) (todoMarkers.ts:143) returns nothing. This is the exact bug just fixed for Trip Templates, where _clean_marked_places (templates.py:85-109) strips dayId/timeOfDay precisely because 'a trip created from a template has brand-new day ids ... those references are dangling'. The clone path was never given the same cleaning.

sharing.title: Clone references the source owner's cover file; the clone's cover 404s when the original owner deletes their trip or changes the cover
sharing.rootCause: _clone_trip_attempt copies src['cover_url'] verbatim (trips.py:1320), so the clone's cover_url points at the ORIGINAL owner's uploaded file under /static/uploads/<owner_id>/<file>. When the owner deletes their trip, delete_trip collects that exact path (collect_trip_upload_paths includes cover_url, helpers.py:78-80) and delete_upload_files physically os.remove()s it (helpers.py:121-163, called at trips.py:455) with NO check for other trips/clones still referencing the file. The same break occurs on cover removal/replacement via upsert_trip. Files are also served read-any to signed-in users, so this is purely a dangling-reference/availability bug, but the clone permanently depends on an asset it doesn't own.

sharing.title: Revoked / expired / archived share page still renders the "I want this trip" clone CTA that then fails with a generic error
sharing.rootCause: The .share-cta block including the clone link (share.html:379-393) is OUTSIDE the {% if days %}/{% if cost %} guards, and the 404 render path in main.py:1085-1101 still passes canonical_url, so the CTA href '/?cloneFromShare=<token>' is emitted on the unavailable page too. clone_trip_from_share_token returns 404 (revoked/deleted) or 410 (archived) (trips.py:1548-1553), and attemptPendingClone surfaces only the generic t('errors.cloneFailedFromCollections') (clone-intent.ts:48-51) with no distinct 'this trip is no longer available' message.

sharing.title: Public share page is hardcoded English despite full en/es/fr/pt app support
sharing.rootCause: share_page (main.py:1072) renders share.html with no locale resolution; <html lang="en"> is hardcoded (share.html:2) and all visible copy is literal English in the template. There is no Accept-Language handling and no server-side string table (unlike the PDF route, which got server-side i18n in PDF-5). The /t/<code> template-preview page shares the same English-only posture, so this is at least consistent, but it remains a real gap for a public, shareable surface.

sharing.title: Bulk /api/sync lets a non-owner planner write trips.is_archived — the share/clone gate's source of truth — diverging from the owner-only mirror the dedicated archive route enforces
sharing.rootCause: The dedicated archive_trip/unarchive_trip routes mirror trips.is_archived ONLY when is_trip_owner is true (trips.py:491-497, 581-587). But the bulk /api/sync active-trips ON CONFLICT writes is_archived=excluded.is_archived unconditionally (data.py:340) from the caller's payload value (data.py:380 `1 if t.get('is_archived') else 0`), gated only by editable_trip_ids — which includes non-owner planners. The first-party frontend now sends an empty /api/sync body so this is dormant for current clients, but the endpoint still accepts a crafted {"trips":[{"id":...,"is_archived":0|1}]} payload from any authenticated editor, and the archive source-of-truth (trips.is_archived) used by the share gate is decoupled from the per-member trip_members.is_archived the rest of the app uses.

media.title: Warm-409 union-merge resurrects a media item the user just deleted (photo / document / checklist to-do / saved place)
media.rootCause: `_mergeMediaField(serverItems, pendingItems)` (media.ts:62) builds `out = [...serverItems]` then appends only local items whose key is NOT already on the server. On a warm write, `persistTripMedia` → `_postTripMedia` sends the FULL post-delete snapshot; the server returns 409 with `current` = live media that STILL CONTAINS the deleted item (because the peer's write didn't delete it). The merge keys by stable `id`, sees the deleted item's id present in `cur`, keeps the server copy, and the local snapshot (which OMITS it = the delete) loses. The merged result is reflected into STATE (media.ts:149-158) and retried, persisting the resurrected item. The code comment at media.ts:42-46 asserts union-by-key 'can't resurrect a peer device's delete' and is only sound for the cold-window ADD-only case — but `_postTripMedia`'s 409 loop runs for EVERY warm write, including deletes, where the assumption is false.

media.title: Warm-409 union-merge silently discards a concurrent EDIT to an existing media item (checklist toggle, doc rename, photo day-reassign, place forManual/forAI toggle)
media.rootCause: Same `_mergeMediaField` (media.ts:62): for an item key present on BOTH sides (the edited item retains its `id`), the server's copy is taken (`out = [...serverItems]`) and the local edited copy is dropped because its key is already `seen`. So 'server wins ties' silently overwrites the local field-level edit. Edits route through `upsertTrip` → `persistTripMedia` → `_postTripMedia`, hitting the same 409 merge. Reorder (tripMediaModals.ts:658-661) is also lost — server item order comes back first, the local reordered array is discarded.

media.title: HEIC→JPEG re-encode exception fallback writes raw HEIC bytes into a .jpg file → broken image served as image/jpeg
media.rootCause: For `.heic`/`.heif`, `filename`/`out_path` are rewritten to `.jpg` at media.py:232-233 BEFORE the encode. The encode `img.save(out_path, format='JPEG', exif=b'')` (media.py:295) can raise — most realistically `OSError: cannot write mode RGBA/P/LA as JPEG` because there is no `img.convert('RGB')` before the JPEG save — and the broad `except Exception:` at media.py:313-315 then does `file.save(out_path)`, persisting the ORIGINAL HEIC bytes under the `.jpg` name. The happy-path fix (rewriting the extension) made the fallback worse, because the fallback no longer matches the file's true format.

media.title: Per-item uploaded files are orphaned on disk on photo/doc removal, cover swap, and document 'Replace'
media.rootCause: Disk cleanup (`collect_trip_upload_paths` + `delete_upload_files`) is wired ONLY into whole-trip and whole-day delete. `update_trip_media` (trips.py:698) just overwrites the photos_json/documents_json column and never diffs the old array to rm the now-unreferenced file; the cover-swap path (trip.ts:463) overwrites `trip.coverUrl` with no cleanup of the prior cover. The 'Replace' document flow (tripMediaModals.ts:856) uploads a new file and overwrites the url, orphaning the old one.

media.title: Media POST does not validate/sanitize item URLs — arbitrary src/url (incl. javascript:) persisted into trip media
media.rootCause: `update_trip_media` (trips.py:719-732) validates only that each field is a list and within the 512KB cap — it never validates the items' URL scheme or ownership, unlike the expense path which runs `validate_upload_url` on `receipt_url`. The client `addTripPhoto`/`addTripDocument` (tripMedia.ts:113, :95) store the raw user-typed string with no scheme allowlist. Cross-user /static/uploads exfiltration is separately blocked by serve_upload's membership gate, but javascript:/data: schemes and external-link abuse are not filtered anywhere.

pdf.title: PDF Expenses 'Category' column prints raw category IDs (UUIDs), not names
pdf.rootCause: _expenses_section renders `cat = e.get('category_id') or e.get('category') or tr('exp_uncategorised')` and prints it raw (_render.py:684,688). Expenses store `categoryId` as the category's `id` (ManualTab.tsx:565 `value={c.id}`), and user-created categories get `id: generateId()` (Personalization.tsx:52) — an opaque UUID, not a slug. The route already builds a category id→name map `_cat_name` for the BUDGET section (__init__.py:1114-1118) but never passes it to `_expenses_section`, and the SELECT for expenses (__init__.py:1153-1158) fetches only `category_id`, not a joined name. The app's own budget-title code (budgets/helpers.ts:226-227) does id-then-name resolution precisely to avoid this; the PDF expenses path skips it.

pdf.title: PDF Settle-up seeds a phantom full-name person for owner-involved settlements, overstating debts vs the in-app screen
pdf.rootCause: Settlements snapshot `from_name`/`to_name` as `users.name` — the FULL display name (settlements.py:338). The roster the PDF builds (and the app uses) keys expenses by the companion's first-name token (the owner is self-stamped under first name). The app's settle math reconciles this via an explicit first-name-token fallback in resolveSettlementParties (balances.ts:107-122). The PDF's `_settle_section` settlement loop (_render.py:840-861) does plain exact-match: `if frm not in bal: bal[frm] = 0.0; bal[frm] += amt` — so a full-name snapshot that doesn't exact-match the roster SEEDS A NEW phantom entry instead of folding into the existing first-name balance. Expenses balance correctly (they use roster names) but the recorded settlement lands on the phantom, leaving the real debt outstanding.

pdf.title: Trip-wide photos tagged to a day are embedded twice (in the day card AND the photo gallery)
pdf.rootCause: The route merges trip-level photos that carry a `dayId` into each day's `photos` list so the day cards render them (__init__.py:1076-1088). But the standalone gallery builds from `trip_photos` = the FULL photos_json list (__init__.py:251 → used at :893 `_collect_photos(trip_photos, ...)`), with no exclusion of the dayId-tagged entries that were just inlined. So a photo with a dayId is collected for both the day card and the gallery.

pdf.title: Per-day map labels and budget scope labels render in English regardless of locale
pdf.rootCause: The whole PDF was localised via the `_STRINGS` table (PDF-5), but these two paths hardcode English literals. __init__.py:505 builds the kicker with a raw f-string + 'PIN'/'PINS'. __init__.py:1119-1130 derives the budget label using literal 'Overall' (:1128), 'Category budget' (:1126,:1130) — even though the i18n table already defines `budget_overall` and `budget_category` keys in all four locales (_i18n.py:62-63 and equivalents), which were clearly added to be used here but never wired up.

pdf.title: Cover 'SPEND' stat truncates the total instead of rounding (€842.90 shows as €842)
pdf.rootCause: `stats.append((f"€{tr.num(int(trip_row['total_spend_eur']), 0)}", ...))` (__init__.py:360) casts to `int()` first, which truncates toward zero, THEN formats with 0 decimals. `tr.num(value, 0)` already rounds-to-nearest via Python format spec, so the extra `int()` both is redundant and forces truncation instead of rounding.

pdf.title: 'Per-day maps' checkbox is silently ignored when 'Day-by-day plan' is unchecked
pdf.rootCause: In the builder, both the overview pins map (__init__.py:469) and the per-day mini-map (__init__.py:523) are nested INSIDE `if opt('includeDays') and days_renderable:` (__init__.py:448). So `includeDayPins` is only honoured when `includeDays` is also true. The modal (modals/pdf.ts:66-69) presents the two as independent toggles, creating a state the backend silently drops.

pdf.title: AI-plan PDF section is unreachable dead code carrying untranslated English (latent)
pdf.rootCause: The frontend modal collects only `input[type=checkbox]` values + `options.locale` (modals/pdf.ts:133-142); no code path populates `options.aiPlan`. The builder's `ai_plan_extra` branch therefore always gets an empty list in production. The dead branch also predates/ignores the PDF-5 i18n work for its inline strings.

collections.title: Collections album covers are gradient placeholders for any trip not opened this session (photos never prefetched for the list)
collections.rootCause: tripCover() (helpers.ts:163-170) falls back coverUrl → trip.photos[0] → day.photos[0]. But /api/data deliberately ships the four heavy media fields empty; api.ts:178 cold-defaults trip.photos=[] for any trip not yet hydrated. fetchTripMedia() is only ever called for the active trip (api.ts:317), on trip-switch (bootstrap/trip-controls.ts:75), and when opening an archived-trip detail (collections.ts:56). It is NEVER called for the Collections list/overview render, so trip.photos is empty there for un-opened trips. Additionally, photos are written to the TRIP-level trip.photos[] array (addTripPhoto with a dayId), not the legacy trip_days.photos column, so tripCover()'s day.photos[0] fallback (helpers.ts:166-167) is also empty for modern trips. Template-instantiated trips have no coverUrl by design (src/routes/templates.py:257-258), so they are always gradient.

collections.title: ArchivedTripDetail shows owner-only Restore + privacy controls on foreign/active trips (dead button + select that persists nothing)
collections.rootCause: ArchivedTripDetail.tsx has no ownership gate — it always renders the Restore button (line 272) and the privacy select (lines 293-303). For foreign trips fetched via /api/public-trip (collections.ts:42 slow path) and for the user's own active trips (fast path), restoreTrip() (handlers.ts:60-62) and toggleTripPrivacy() (handlers.ts:36-37) both look the trip up in STATE.archivedTrips/STATE.trips and early-return when not found, so they no-op. The privacy <select> is uncontrolled (defaultValue), so its displayed value changes on the user's pick even though nothing persisted. The public-trip endpoint already returns ownerId on the trip (src/routes/public.py:185), so an ownership check is feasible.

collections.title: ArchivedCard list/drill-in cards render only coverUrl, never the photo fallback — inconsistent with the album overview and detail hero
collections.rootCause: ArchivedCard only renders trip.coverUrl (Collections.tsx:494 `{trip.coverUrl && }`) with no fallback. The album overview uses tripCover() (helpers.ts:163, which adds the trip.photos / day.photos fallback chain) and the detail hero uses the same chain (ArchivedTripDetail.tsx:91-99). So the card list is the odd one out — it ignores trip.photos entirely.

collections.title: Year-album shelf is not in chronological order under the default 'Recent' sort
collections.rootCause: groupTrips() (helpers.ts:187-207) orders albums by first appearance of each key in the already-sorted trip list. The default sort is 'recent' (state.ts:39), i.e. archivedAt-desc, so year-album order tracks the order in which each year's most-recently-archived trip appears — which is unrelated to the year value. Verified by simulation: trips archived in order across years 2021/2024/2021/2023 produce shelf order [2021, 2024, 2023].

collections.title: Hardcoded English strings in Collections / archived-detail surfaces (Sort/Filter titles, view-count, share tooltips)
collections.rootCause: These literals are inlined rather than passed through t(). It is also internally inconsistent: the neighbouring Group-by select correctly uses t('collections.groupByLabel') for its title/aria (Collections.tsx:288-289), while the Sort/year/destination selects right next to it are hardcoded.

collections.title: 'Most days' sort counts the day-0 Trip Hub while the card day count excludes it
