collections.rootCause: The daysDesc sort uses (a.tripDays?.length || 0) (helpers.ts:131-135), which includes the day-0 Hub anchor. The card's displayed day count uses plannedDays = tripDays.filter(d => dayNumber > 0).length (Collections.tsx:480), explicitly excluding the Hub (per BUG-33). The two counts diverge whenever Hub rows differ (e.g. a duplicate day-0 that hasn't been self-healed yet).

feed-social.title: Block primitive has no UI entry point — entire server-enforced safety feature is unreachable
feed-social.rootCause: The frontend helper `blockUser(userId)` (api/feed.ts:31) is defined but NEVER invoked anywhere in the app — `grep` shows the only importers are api/feed.ts (definition) and Blocks.tsx (which imports only `fetchBlockedUsers` + `unblockUser`). Profile.tsx renders only `FollowButton` for foreign profiles (Profile.tsx:718-725); there is no Block control. Comments in Blocks.tsx:6 and Settings.tsx:347 reference 'a stray Block tap on a profile card' that does not exist in the codebase — the trigger was never built (or was removed) while all the server enforcement (blocks.py, follows.py:104, friends.py:155, feed_events.py block filters, explore block filter) shipped.

feed-social.title: Explore feed block filter is one-directional — leaks blocked-by users' public-trip cards into the blocker-target's Explore
feed-social.rootCause: The explore_feed query only excludes trips owned by users the CALLER has blocked: `AND t.user_id NOT IN (SELECT blocked_id FROM blocks WHERE blocker_id = ?)` (feed.py:456-458). It omits the reverse direction (`SELECT blocker_id FROM blocks WHERE blocked_id = ?`). Compare build_feed_context (feed_events.py:1117-1122) and public.py:553-554, which both filter both directions. The inline comment at feed.py:430 even claims 'block-aware Explore' but only implemented one side.

feed-social.title: Comment count vs. comment list desync after a block (third-party posts)
feed-social.rootCause: `list_feed_comments` filters out blocked authors: `AND c.user_id NOT IN (SELECT blocked_id FROM blocks WHERE blocker_id = ?)` (feed.py:1135-1145). But `_attach_engagement_counts` computes comment_count with a plain `SELECT event_id, COUNT(*) FROM feed_comments ... GROUP BY event_id` (feed.py:181-186) with NO block filter. The block sweep in blocks.py:148-162 only deletes the blocked user's engagement on the BLOCKER'S OWN feed_posts, never on a third party's post, so B's comment on C's share survives and inflates the count for A.

feed-social.title: Like count includes blocked users' likes on third-party shares
feed-social.rootCause: `_attach_engagement_counts` likes query (feed.py:163-168) does `SELECT event_id, COUNT(*) FROM feed_likes ... GROUP BY event_id` with no block filter, and the block sweep (blocks.py:148-162) only removes the blocked user's likes on the blocker's OWN posts, not on third-party posts.

feed-social.title: Following a blocked user from Friends search shows a false 'Request sent!' success
feed-social.rootCause: search_friends only excludes users who blocked the CALLER (friends.py:87), not users the caller blocked, so B still appears. `_follow` correctly no-ops on a block edge (friends.py:155-156, returns False), but add_friend ignores the return value and always returns `{status:'success'}` (friends.py:207-209). Friends.tsx:238 then sets searchStatus 'sent' on status==='success'.

feed-social.title: Follows have no per-account daily cap — open notification-spam fan-out channel
feed-social.rootCause: The limiter is keyed on get_remote_address (extensions.py:24), and follow_user/add_friend have only the per-IP @limiter.limit('60/minute') with NO `user_daily_count` gate. The per-account daily-cap pattern exists for feed_comment (feed.py:1189, 200/day) and trip_create (trips.py:129, 50/day) but was never applied to follows, which the follows.py docstring itself flags as a bell-spam / 'petty follower-count games' risk.

feed-social.title: Explore tab is entirely hardcoded English (no i18n)
feed-social.rootCause: ExploreSection.tsx (lines 49, 61-63, 106, 144-159) and ExploreCard.tsx (line 54) use raw English string literals instead of `t(...)`. The rest of the Feed (Feed.tsx, Friends.tsx) is properly routed through the i18n table, so this tab is an inconsistent gap.

feed-social.title: Feed bundle lines and several event verbs are hardcoded English (i18n gap)
feed-social.rootCause: `bundleLine` (render.ts:186-206) returns entirely hardcoded English template strings with no t() call. In `eventLine`, the achievement_unlocked branch (render.ts:326-332) hardcodes 'unlocked' and 'a new badge', and the settled_up branch (render.ts:333-354) hardcodes 'settled up with', 'on', 'a trip', and the 'someone' fallback. Action buttons' title/aria attrs (render.ts:441,463,472,481) are also hardcoded ('Like','Unlike','Comments','Repost to your friends','Bookmark').

feed-social.title: FollowButton, Blocks tab, and profile Log-Out labels are hardcoded English
feed-social.rootCause: FollowButton.tsx:85 returns the literal 'Following'/'Follow' (its error toast uses t() but the button label does not). Blocks.tsx:59-63,140,169 use literal English ('Unblock this user?', 'Unblock', 'Unknown user'); the file's own comment notes the relative-time was i18n'd but the modal/labels were missed. Profile.tsx:713 hardcodes 'Log Out'.

notifications.title: Account deletion / factory-reset fails to clean orphaned engagement notifications (wrong type names + wrong key column)
notifications.rootCause: The factory-reset sweep at data.py:1430-1436 deletes engagement-notification orphans with `... AND type IN ('feed_liked','feed_commented','feed_reposted','feed_bookmarked')` and matches on `related_id IN (doomed_post_ids)`. BOTH are wrong: (1) those four type strings do not exist anywhere in the codebase — the real engagement notification types are 'share_liked'/'share_commented'/'share_reposted' (feed.py:122-129); (2) engagement notifications store the POST in the `post_id` column and the ACTOR's user_id in `related_id` (feed.py:133-135), so filtering by related_id can never match a post id. Net: the DELETE matches zero rows. The single-post delete (feed.py:806) and trip delete (trips.py:420) do it correctly with `DELETE FROM notifications WHERE post_id IN (...)`; the bulk reset path is the broken sibling — the very orphan class the migration f5a6b7c8d9e0 (NF3) added post_id to fix.

notifications.title: Trip-invite notification is never cleared after Accept/Decline — bell badge stuck unread forever, re-click errors
notifications.rootCause: handleNotificationClick deliberately SKIPS markNotificationRead for type==='trip_invite' (notifications.ts:287-290) with the comment 'the accept/decline routes already remove the notification server-side'. But respond_trip_invite (trips.py:962) never deletes the invitee's trip_invite notification — it only flips/deletes the trip_members row and notifies the INVITER. The modal handlers (share.ts:52-83) call respondTripInvite then close(), with no client-side mark-read or removal either (feed.ts:279-285 is a bare POST). So the notification is both un-read-marked on the client AND un-deleted on the server.

notifications.title: Self-leaving a trip sends the leaver a notification saying they removed themselves
notifications.rootCause: remove_trip_member supports self-leave (is_self_leave = actor == target, trips.py:1089) and only uses that flag to skip the planner permission gate. The trip_member_removed notification insert at trips.py:1148-1152 is unconditional and addressed to `target` — which equals the actor on self-leave — with message built from actor_name (the leaver's own name). There is no `if not is_self_leave:` guard around the notification.

notifications.title: Notification titles/bodies for settled_up, settled_up_reverted and achievement_unlocked are hardcoded English for all locales
notifications.rootCause: Notification titles render as `n.title || notificationDefaultTitle(n.type)` (notifications.ts:241). The server always inserts a non-null English title (settlements.py 'Settled up'/'Settlement reverted', achievements.py 'Achievement unlocked'), so notificationDefaultTitle's t() fallback never runs — and it has no case for these types anyway. localizeNotificationMessage (notifications.ts:122-154) has handlers for the common types but NONE for settled_up_reverted or achievement_unlocked, and its settled_up regex only matches the simple '{from} settled {amount} {currency} with you for {trip}.' form, not the recorder-variant body — so those bodies stay English. The i18n-drift test (tests/test_i18n_drift.py) only checks en.ts values against es/fr/pt; it cannot see server-side Python strings that never have t() keys.

notifications.title: Following someone via the Friends page emits a 'Friend Request' notification whose body says 'started following you'
notifications.rootCause: Friends.tsx:232 still posts to the legacy /api/friends/add, whose handler calls _follow(..., source='friend_request') (friends.py:207). _follow inserts a notification with type='friend_request' and title 'Friend Request' when source != 'followed_you' (friends.py:179-181), while the message is always 'started following you.'. The live /api/follows/<id> path (used by the profile FollowButton) correctly uses type='followed_you' + title 'New follower'; the Friends page uses the legacy façade, producing the mismatched 'Friend Request' label. The title is the server's literal string, so the frontend's t()-based title fallback never localizes it.

notifications.title: settled_up / settled_up_reverted notification clicks dump the user on Home instead of the trip's Settlements
notifications.rootCause: handleNotificationClick's switch has no case for 'settled_up' or 'settled_up_reverted', so both fall to `default: navigate(PAGES.HOME)` (notifications.ts:352-354). The function's own docstring (notifications.ts:261-263) states related_id is a trip_id for settled_up/settled_up_reverted, so the data needed to route to the trip's settlement page is present but unused.

notifications.title: clearLocalState resets STATE.notifications but not STATE.notificationsTotalUnread (badge-count state diverges from documented full-reset invariant)
notifications.rootCause: core.ts:118 sets `STATE.notifications = []` (and resets 8 other fields) but never resets notificationsTotalUnread. The parallel explicit-logout path in profile.ts DOES reset it (profile.ts:80), proving the omission is an oversight, not intent. The leak is largely masked because updateUserUI toggles the 'is-signed-out' body class (hiding the bell) when STATE.user is null, and the 401 path emits state:changed → updateUserUI; but the field still carries stale cross-user state that any future code reading it (or a render before the class applies) would surface.

notifications.title: Unread notifications are never cleaned up — unbounded growth behind the LIMIT 50 window
notifications.rootCause: The maintenance sweep deletes only `is_read = 1 AND created_at < -30 days` (main.py:786-789). Unread rows are intentionally preserved ('so the user's bell keeps surfacing them'), but with no age ceiling and a 50-row read cap, ancient unread notifications neither surface nor get reclaimed.

settings.title: Changing language from Settings leaves the page's own chrome in the old language (partial repaint)
settings.rootCause: setLocale() (i18n.ts:157) writes STATE.preferences.locale + emit('state:changed') but never re-mounts or re-navigates. In Settings.tsx the top-level Settings() component subscribes ONLY to the tab store (useSettingsTabSnapshot), and MenuView()/SubTabStrip() subscribe to no store at all. React only re-renders a component when one of ITS OWN hooks fires, so only the store-subscribed sub-sections (GeneralLanguageSection/GeneralAppearanceSection via useStore) repaint on a locale change; the surrounding chrome that renders t() strings does not. paintI18nBindings (main.ts:178) only repaints legacy [data-i18n-key] DOM nodes, not React text. The Personalization page does NOT have this bug because Personalization() calls useStore at its top, so its whole subtree repaints.

settings.title: Sessions and Blocked-users settings panels are entirely hardcoded English (no i18n)
settings.rootCause: Both panels were shipped with the translation pass deliberately skipped. Sessions.tsx admits it in a comment (lines 118-120: 'keys for these strings can be added … for now we hard-code English'); _deviceLabel/_formatRelativeTime, the confirm messages (lines 93-98), the headings (123-127), the 'This device' chip (168), and the button labels (200) are literals. Blocks.tsx uses only 4 t() calls and otherwise hard-codes its strings. The verified locale tables (en/es/fr/pt) are key-complete (1540 keys each, 0 missing), so these surfaces simply never route through t().

settings.title: Revoked creator can no longer delete their own Trip Templates (delete gated on is_creator before ownership)
settings.rootCause: delete_template (templates.py:482) checks `if not _is_creator(cursor, user_id): return 403` (line 486) BEFORE running the ownership-scoped `DELETE … WHERE id=? AND owner_id=?` (line 488). Deleting your OWN content should require ownership, not the (separately revocable) can-CREATE privilege. set_creator (admin.py) only flips the flag and intentionally leaves templates live, but combined with this gate the user is locked out of managing their own published content after revocation.

settings.title: Factory Reset wipes data but silently re-logs the user in (HttpOnly session cookie survives)
settings.rootCause: confirmResetApp (Settings.tsx:186) DELETEs /api/user-data, clears local STATE (including STATE.user = null, line 210), then does localStorage.clear() + location.reload() (lines 214-215). The session JWT lives in an HttpOnly `gg_session` cookie that JS cannot clear and that only /api/auth/logout invalidates (api/core.ts:57). location.reload() therefore re-runs restoreSession(), which succeeds on the surviving cookie, so the user is re-authenticated. The server data is genuinely wiped (so this is not data corruption), but the behavior contradicts the 'Erase Everything' framing and the explicit STATE.user=null.

settings.title: Edit-Category modal mixes localized and hardcoded-English strings
settings.rootCause: openEditCategoryModal (settings.ts:95) builds the modal via showModal innerHTML with hardcoded literals: the <h2> 'Edit Category' (line 106), the input placeholder='Category name' (line 110), and the Cancel button text (line 115). Only the submit button uses t('settings.editCategorySaveBtn') (line 114). This legacy helper was left in settings.ts during the React migration and never fully i18n'd.

settings.title: Theme and POI customization are device-local while Language syncs across devices, despite identical Settings presentation
settings.rootCause: STATE.preferences (theme, mapDefaultPois, poiFilters, poiVisible, poiAnchoring, pillEpicenters, enabledPois) is persisted ONLY to localStorage (state.ts:59 'Stored on this device only'; subscribe(STATE_CHANGED, saveState) at state.ts:438). The server has no preferences column. Language is special-cased: setLocale (i18n.ts:192) additionally POSTs /api/profile/update {language}, stored in users.language and hydrated into STATE.preferences.locale on boot (main.ts:123-129). So locale is the only cross-device preference; theme/POI silently are not. The theme/POI copy doesn't promise sync, so this is an inconsistency rather than a broken promise, but the parallel UI invites the wrong expectation.

settings.title: Admin dashboard: per-user trip count includes archived trips while global total excludes them
settings.rootCause: The global totalTrips (admin.py:136-141) counts only NON-archived trips (JOIN trip_members on the owner row WHERE COALESCE(is_archived,0)=0). The per-user roster's trip_count (admin.py:202) is `(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM trips t WHERE t.user_id = u.id)` — every owned trip regardless of archive state. The two definitions diverge for any user with archived trips.

i18n.title: Day-Journaling modal is 100% hardcoded English (title, prompt, placeholder, buttons, toast) — no i18n at all
i18n.rootCause: journalingModal.ts never imports `t` and emits raw English literals in its innerHTML and showLiquidAlert call. It is the only home-modal that was never migrated to i18n (dayDetailModal.ts has ~40 t() calls, tripMediaModals.ts ~64, tripChecklistModal.ts is fully localized). No locale keys exist for any of these strings (grep for 'Save Story'/'Memories saved'/'Capture your memories' in en.ts returns nothing). It is reachably wired from TripBody.tsx:305-307 via the `.day-journaling-btn` delegate.

i18n.title: Day-pin map toasts ('Click on the map…', 'Location saved!', 'Day deleted') are hardcoded English
i18n.rootCause: handlers.ts imports `t` (line 28) and uses it elsewhere, but these three showLiquidAlert() calls pass bare English string literals. No locale keys exist for them (grep for 'Location saved'/'Click on the map'/'Day deleted' in en.ts returns nothing).

i18n.title: PDF export modal: 'Expenses', 'Settle up', 'Photos' option labels + descriptions hardcoded in English (acknowledged shortcut)
i18n.rootCause: Deliberate shortcut documented in-code at modals/pdf.ts:74-77 ('The modal labels are English literals because the brief forbids touching the locale files; the PDF CONTENT is localised server-side'). The renderPdfOption() calls at lines 78-83 pass raw English strings instead of t(). The localized PDF body does not cover this client-side modal UI.

i18n.title: Trip-picker shows hardcoded English 'No Active Trips' in navbar/sidebar when user has no trips
i18n.rootCause: updateTripSelector() runs on every state:changed and overwrites the select's innerHTML with `'<option value="">No Active Trips</option>'` (trip-controls.ts:45) — a hardcoded literal, so even a data-i18n attribute on the static option would be clobbered. The module imports `t` (used at line 93+) but not for this string; no key exists for it. The index.html `<option>Select a Trip...</option>` (lines 513/543) also has no data-i18n-key.

i18n.title: Localized population-fact templates produce ungrammatical 'thousand' phrasing in ES/PT/FR ('77 mil de personas', '77 mille d'habitants')
i18n.rootCause: localizeFact() (place-names.ts:30-41) re-formats the parsed fact through a SINGLE template (facts.country/facts.state) that hardcodes the partitive particle ('de {unit} de personas' / '{unit} d'habitants') which is grammatically correct only for the 'million' unit. The 'thousand' unit (facts.unitThousand = 'mil'/'mil'/'mille') does not take the particle in these languages, so the fixed template is wrong for every thousand-scale country.

i18n.title: Home-hero inspirational travel quotes are hardcoded English for the no-trip slideshow (all locales)
i18n.rootCause: In the no-trip branch, slideshow.ts:94 sets `quotes = INSPIRATIONAL_PAIRS.map(p => p.q)` and renders them verbatim — there is no localization path for `.q` (unlike the active-trip path, which runs facts through localizeFact). The quotes live as English literals in constants.ts:69-82 / TRAVEL_DATA_DEFAULT (constants.ts:85). The active-trip path intentionally ignores `.q` (slideshow.ts:162-168), so only the no-trip path leaks.

i18n.title: Systematic hardcoded English in React title/aria-label/alt attributes across ~15 components
i18n.rootCause: These components localize their visible text/options via t() but left the title=, aria-label=, and one alt= attribute as English string literals. The repo is otherwise disciplined (no hardcoded placeholder=); these attributes were missed. For sighted users the title= tooltips are visibly English; for screen-reader users the aria-labels are announced in English even after <html lang> is switched, so VoiceOver/NVDA pronounce them with the wrong-language phonemes.

i18n.title: Hardcoded English error toasts in Feed and Archived-trip share (bypass t())
i18n.rootCause: Both call showLiquidAlert() with bare English literals / template strings instead of routing through t(). The app already has parallel localized toast keys (e.g. errors.commentPostFailed, errors.deleteFailed) but these two paths were not migrated.

i18n.title: Batch-import provider option labels ('Tricount Export (CSV/XLSX)', 'Splitwise Export') hardcoded English in the dropdown
i18n.rootCause: POPULARS is a module-level array (BatchUpload.tsx:27-30) with hardcoded English `name` values; the rest of the component uses t(). Brand names ('Tricount'/'Splitwise') should stay, but the descriptive 'Export (CSV/XLSX)' / 'Export' suffixes are untranslated.

i18n.title: a11y skip-link 'Skip to content' is hardcoded English (no data-i18n binding)
i18n.rootCause: The skip-link in index.html has no data-i18n-key attribute, so paintI18nBindings (which hydrates [data-i18n-key] nodes) never localizes it. Every other nav label in index.html carries a data-i18n-key; this one was missed.

i18n.title: Capital/biggest-city names baked into fact data use non-localized exonyms ('Wien', 'Alger') shown in all locales
i18n.rootCause: localizeFact (place-names.ts:36-41) only localizes the surrounding template; the {capital}/{biggest} slot values come verbatim from the English source data in constants.ts, which itself contains inconsistent exonyms ('Wien', 'Alger'). There is no per-language city-name table (intentional, to avoid ~960 strings), so the data's own inconsistency leaks to every locale.

sync-offline.title: Offline outbox dedup keys on (method, url) only — collapses ALL offline edits of one resource type to a single row; the rest are silently lost
sync-offline.rootCause: enqueueMutation dedupes by `i.method === item.method && i.url === item.url` (outbox.ts:205-207). But every row-upsert POST uses a CONSTANT url with the row id in the BODY, not the URL: upsertExpense→POST /api/expenses, upsertDay→/api/days, upsertBudget→/api/budgets, upsertTrip→/api/trips, createSettlement→/api/settlements (confirmed server-side: expenses.py:47 / trips.py:82 read id from the body). So two different expenses both dedupe to key (POST,/api/expenses) and the second REPLACES the first's body (outbox.ts:208-218 keeps prev.id/enqueuedAt but takes the NEW body). Offline nothing drains, so all offline mutations pile up and collapse. The R8-B1 dedup was written to coalesce repeated edits of the SAME row but the key can't distinguish rows. DELETE is unaffected (id is in the URL: /api/expenses/<id>).

sync-offline.title: Offline batch CSV/Tricount/Splitwise import: entire import collapses to ONE expense in the outbox
sync-offline.rootCause: applyImport loops `STATE.expenses.push(expense); void upsertExpense(expense)` per row (upload.ts:440-446) with no online gate. All rows fire near-simultaneously; offline they all enqueue to the same (POST,/api/expenses) outbox key, which dedup-collapses to the last one (see the dedup-key finding). There is no `navigator.onLine` guard on import (unlike the settle form at pages/settlement/actions.ts:111).

sync-offline.title: Offline media deletes (to-do place removal, 'Clear all', photo/document delete) are silently undone — union-merge can only ADD items back, never remove
sync-offline.rootCause: When a trip's media isn't hydrated in THIS tab (`_mediaLoadedTrips` is in-memory and empty after every page reload), persistTripMedia PARKS the snapshot in `_pendingMedia` and never enqueues an outbox write (media.ts:274-286). On reconnect, fetchTripMedia union-merges the parked snapshot onto server media via _mergeMediaField, which is `out = [...serverItems]` then appends only parked items the server lacks (media.ts:62-73) — it can ONLY add, never remove. A delete is represented as an ABSENCE in the parked snapshot, so the still-present server item survives the union and is resurrected. The TRIP-1 comment's assumption 'the only possible edit is an ADD — nothing is loaded yet to delete' is false: STATE.trips media (checklist/markedPlaces/photos) is restored from localStorage by loadState() (state.ts:136), so the user can fully see and delete items while _mediaLoadedTrips is empty. 'Clear all' (markedPlaces=[]) parks an empty array → union with server = every server place restored.

sync-offline.title: Service worker calls skipWaiting() on every install — the 'Update available, reload?' prompt is dead code and tabs auto-reload mid-edit
sync-offline.rootCause: install handler unconditionally calls `self.skipWaiting()` (sw.js:89) and activate calls `self.clients.claim()` (sw.js:104). skipWaiting promotes the new SW straight past the 'waiting' state, so `registration.waiting` is (almost) never populated — and the entire tryPrompt/showConfirmModal flow in main.ts:394-431 gates on `registration.waiting`, so it never fires. Instead clients.claim() triggers `controllerchange`, and main.ts:443-447 reloads immediately (guarded only by a once-flag, with no `navigator.serviceWorker.controller` first-install check), so updates apply with a surprise reload and first load double-loads.

sync-offline.title: Outbox is never drained on the 15s poll or on tab re-focus — a mutation that fails while ONLINE (e.g. a 20s timeout) stays stuck until the next offline→online transition or a full reload
sync-offline.rootCause: drainOutbox() is only invoked from the `online` event listener (main.ts:319) and a one-shot 2s boot timer (main.ts:333). The recurring 15s poll (main.ts:261-269) calls only syncWithServer + fetchNotifications, and the visibilitychange/pageshow handlers (main.ts:283-301) likewise never drain. apiFetch enqueues on ANY caught failure including the 20s timeout (core.ts:218,251), so an online-but-slow request becomes a stuck queue item with no trigger to retry.

sync-offline.title: No pending-changes indicator exists — offline queued writes (and their silent dedup loss) are completely invisible to the user
sync-offline.rootCause: pendingCount/listPending (outbox.ts:229-238) have no consumer anywhere in pages/ or components/. Offline saves go through the optimistic path + outbox, but the only feedback is a misleading 'Save failed' (red) toast because upsertExpense returns {ok:false,status:0} on network failure. Combined with the dedup data-loss bugs, the user gets no warning that their offline work is at risk.

sync-offline.title: drainOutbox drops a 4xx (401/403) replay silently with no user feedback — offline edits by a since-demoted/kicked member, or after session expiry, vanish without a toast
sync-offline.rootCause: _drainOutboxImpl treats any 4xx other than 409 as a permanent client error and removes the item with only `console.warn` (outbox.ts:369-380). Unlike the inline upsert path (which toasts staleEdit on 409 and clears the session on 401 via apiFetch), the drain path has no user-facing surface, so the loss is invisible.

state.title: 401 session-expiry re-persists the logged-out user's trips to localStorage (privacy wipe defeated by the same handler's emit)
state.rootCause: clearAuthToken() (api/core.ts:82) does localStorage.removeItem('theGreatEscapeState') and resets a SUBSET of STATE (notifications, draftExpense, geminiApiKey, settlements, achievements, lastImportBatch, preferences, rateMode, insightCurrency) but NOT the heavy domain arrays (STATE.trips/expenses/budgets/tripDays/archivedTrips/categories). The 401 branch in apiFetch (api/core.ts:269-284) calls clearAuthToken() then STATE.user=null then emit(EVENTS.STATE_CHANGED). The default subscriber `subscribe(EVENTS.STATE_CHANGED, saveState)` (state.ts:438) schedules a debounced _doSave() (state.ts:353-367) which JSON.stringify(STATE)s the WHOLE object — including the trips still in memory — back into the key clearAuthToken just removed. The full logout path (pages/profile.ts:70-89) clears these arrays itself, which is why it's safe; the 401 + restoreSession()-failure callers of clearAuthToken (api/core.ts:275, bootstrap/auth.ts:193) rely on clearAuthToken to do the wipe, and it doesn't finish the job.

state.title: Trip-media hydration Maps (_mediaLoadedTrips/_mediaVersion/_pendingMedia) survive logout → shared-device media loss + hidden media on shared trips
state.rootCause: _mediaLoadedTrips (Set), _mediaVersion (Map) and _pendingMedia (Map) are module-level singletons in api/media.ts and are NEVER cleared on logout — clearAuthToken() (api/core.ts:82) and pages/profile.ts logout reset STATE + the SW cache but never call .clear() on these. They key purely by tripId, with no user binding, so a tripId User A loaded stays 'loaded' for User B. Two consequences chain: (a) pullFromServer's cold-hydrate guard (api.ts:317) treats T as already hydrated for B and never GETs the real media, leaving B's STATE on the []-placeholder; (b) persistTripMedia's safety gate (media.ts:274), which exists specifically to stop []-placeholders from clobbering server media, is bypassed because the stale set says T is hydrated — so the placeholder write proceeds.

state.title: Warm-path 409 union-merge resurrects a peer device's deleted media item
state.rootCause: _postTripMedia's 409 recovery (media.ts:139-144) re-merges via _mergeMediaField (media.ts:62-73), a pure union-by-key that keeps any key present on EITHER side. The cold/parked path's loss-free claim relies on 'the only possible offline edit is an ADD' (media.ts:46), but the warm 409 path runs on a fully-hydrated trip where a DELETE is a normal edit. Union-by-key cannot represent a deletion, so a concurrent delete always loses to any concurrent edit. There is no tombstone/per-item version to distinguish 'never had it' from 'deleted it'.

state.title: Per-trip POI preference keys (enabledPois / pillEpicenters / poiAnchoring) are never pruned when a trip is deleted
state.rootCause: The permanent-delete handler (trip-controls.ts:174-204) prunes expenses, tripDays, settlements, budgets, and STATE.fxOverridesByTrip[trip.id] (line 192) but NOT STATE.preferences.enabledPois[trip.id] / pillEpicenters[trip.id]. The archived-trip permanent-delete path (collections/handlers.ts:94) prunes none of the per-trip preference maps at all. There is no boot-time reconciliation that drops preference keys whose tripId no longer exists in STATE.trips/archivedTrips (loadState only backfills/heals these maps, never garbage-collects stale keys — state.ts:189-207).

state.title: cpiCache / rateCache are persisted but not validated or non-finite-healed on load (NaN can reach the inflation factor), unlike the sibling money maps
state.rootCause: validateLoadedState (schemas.ts:198-225) only shape-checks top-level arrays + activeTripId + user; it does not inspect rateCache/cpiCache. loadState() adds explicit non-finite healing loops for STATE.fxOverridesByTrip (state.ts:146-165) and STATE.manualRates (state.ts:170-188) — with a comment that a corrupt override 'could carry NaN/Infinity into the Insights calc and poison every total' — but cpiCache/rateCache, which feed the identical calc, get no equivalent heal. The runtime guards in presentValue.ts cover the override (ovValid, IA-1) and manual (Number.isFinite(manualPct)) branches but not the auto CPI branch, where makeInflationFactor only guards latestVal !== 0, not interior values.

security.title: Any trip member (incl. Relaxer) can fabricate settlements between two OTHER members, corrupting everyone's shared balance graph, and cannot undo their own
security.rootCause: create_settlement() deliberately does not require the caller to be one of the two parties ('the caller does NOT have to be one of the two parties', settlements.py:144-152). The only gate is `_is_accepted_member(cursor, trip_id, user_id)` (line 233), which returns True for every role including Relaxer. The amount cap (total_spend*1.01, line 305) bounds the size but not the act of fabrication. The delete authorization (line 521: `user_id != owner_id and user_id != row['from_user_id']`) excludes the recorder, so a third-party who records cannot retract it.

security.title: POST /api/settlements does not re-verify the two parties are STILL accepted members at read time, and snapshots names only — a kicked member's fabricated settlement persists in the balance graph
security.rootCause: Settlements are immutable post-creation and /api/data (data.py:1178-1184) selects ALL settlements for the trip with no re-check that from_user_id/to_user_id are still members. remove_trip_member (trips.py:1103) deletes the trip_members row but does not sweep or flag the member's settlements.

security.title: /api/sync expense and trip_days loops KeyError → uncaught 500 on a bulk payload entry missing `id` or `tripId`
security.rootCause: The per-row loops use subscript access (`e['id']`, `e['tripId']`, `d['id']`, `d['tripId']`) instead of `.get()` with a skip/continue, unlike the per-row delta endpoints which validate id presence and return a clean 400. The SEC-2 hardening covered non-dict BODIES but not missing per-row keys inside the bulk arrays.

security.title: DELETE /api/expenses/<id> is the only mutating route with no per-route rate limit (relies solely on the 200/min global default)
security.rootCause: Missing @limiter.limit decorator on delete_expense; the global default_limits=['200 per minute'] (extensions.py:25) silently covers it but at a much higher rate than intended for a write path.

security.title: upsert_expense uses `request.json or {}` instead of the SEC-2 json_body() helper, so a POST without application/json content-type 415s/400s before the isinstance guard
security.rootCause: The SEC-2 sweep (json_body across write routes) missed expenses.py — it kept the legacy `request.json or {}` idiom that the helper was introduced to replace.

security.title: /components design-system gallery is served publicly with no auth in production
security.rootCause: components_preview() has no @require_auth and no dev-env gate (unlike the JWT-secret and rate-limit guards which check _is_dev_env()). It renders templates/components.html unconditionally.

home.title: Stored XSS on Home dashboard via unescaped trip name in the 'expenses recorded for {trip}' stat line
home.rootCause: HomeHeader renders the stat line with `dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: t('home.tripStatsLine', { count: 'N', trip: activeTrip.name || '' }) }}`. The t() interpolator does a plain String(params[name]) substitution with NO HTML-escaping (i18n.ts:266-267), and the {trip} slot is filled with the raw user-controlled trip name. The template uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML only to bold the count, but the same unescaped path carries activeTrip.name into the DOM as live HTML. Server-side, upsert_trip binds t['name'] straight into the INSERT with only a presence check (trips.py:88) and no sanitization, and it round-trips raw via /api/data. Sibling code escapes the name correctly — trip-controls.ts:52 uses esc(t.name), TripBody.tsx:974/979 wrap with esc(...), Collections.tsx uses plain JSX {trip.name} — so this is an inconsistency that singled out the Home stat line.

home.title: No map placeholder or error state when Google Maps fails to load — active-trip hero is a blank glass card
home.rootCause: HeroMap's effect bails when typeof google === 'undefined' || !google.maps and calls whenGoogleMapsReady() (which rejects after a 10s timeout, googleMapsServices.ts:56-57). The rejection path only console.warns (HeroMap.tsx:162-164); nothing renders a placeholder. The #homeHeroMap div stays empty. The card is .cover-card cover-card--md, and .cover-card--md (home.css:483) sets only height + border/radius — no background-color — so the empty map area is just the parent .glass translucent blur. There is a country-select fallback for Maps-unavailable in the trip-create modal (task MK3-1) but the active-trip hero map has no equivalent.

home.title: Personalized greeting silently always says 'traveler' — server never sends user.firstName
home.rootCause: pickGreeting reads STATE.user.firstName (welcomeCard.ts:70) and falls back to 'traveler' when it's missing. But the server's user payload in EVERY auth endpoint (/api/me at auth.py:67-77, dev-login at 185-187, Google login at 262-264) only sends name, never firstName. The client assigns STATE.user = data.user verbatim (auth.ts:58), so user.firstName is always undefined. The rest of the codebase derives the first name from name (e.g. modals/trip.ts:160 STATE.user.name.split(' ')[0]), so welcomeCard.ts is the odd one out reading a field that's never populated.

home.title: Fresh-trip greeting re-rolls (text changes) on any background state change because the random pick runs in render
home.rootCause: HomeHeader calls pickGreeting(activeTrip, isFresh) directly in render (HomeHeader.tsx:64), and pickGreeting picks one of four templates with Math.random() each call (welcomeCard.ts:79). Home subscribes via useActiveTrip → useStore, which re-renders on every state:changed emit (store.ts bumps a version counter and useSyncExternalStore re-renders all subscribers). So any real STATE change re-runs the random pick and the H1 text changes. It only happens while isFresh is true (once a day/expense exists, pickGreeting returns the stable greetingDefault), which bounds the window — but it's a jarring, non-deterministic flicker during onboarding, the exact moment polish matters most.

home.title: No-trip welcome slideshow shows a broken-image glyph (empty alt) when Unsplash is unreachable
home.rootCause: The slideshow image element is emitted as `` (welcomeCard.ts:36) with no onerror fallback, and the 6s rotation just swaps imgEl.src (slideshow.ts:233) with no error handling. Every slideshow image is a hardcoded https://images.unsplash.com/... URL (constants.INSPIRATIONAL_PAIRS). When the fetch fails the browser shows its default broken-image placeholder; the empty alt means there isn't even descriptive text. (The active-trip hero is a map, not an img, so this is specific to the no-trip welcome path.)

a11y.title: New-Trip modal: 'Trip name', 'Destination' & template-code inputs have no programmatic label (screen reader announces blank fields)
a11y.rootCause: The `<label class="form-label">` elements for trip name (line 48) and destination (line 52) carry NO `for`/`htmlFor` attribute and do not wrap the input, so there is no programmatic association; the inputs (#tripName, #tripPlaceInput) also have no `aria-label`. The template-code `<input id="templateCodeInput">` (line 41) has only a placeholder. By contrast the date label on line 62 correctly uses `for="tripDateRange"`, so the pattern was known but not applied to the text inputs. The route-level axe gate (tests/e2e/a11y.spec.js) never opens this modal, so the violation is uncaught.

a11y.title: Edit-Trip modal: 'Adventure name' & 'Destination' inputs have unassociated labels (same label gap as New-Trip)
a11y.rootCause: `<label class="form-label">` on lines 253, 257 (and 277) have no `for` and don't wrap their inputs (#editTripName, #editTripPlaceInput, #editTripCoverInput); inputs carry no `aria-label`. Only the date label (line 264) uses `for=`. WCAG 1.3.1 / 4.1.2 (label / button-name) fail. Uncaught because the route-only axe gate never opens this modal.

a11y.title: Add-Day modal: 'Where' and 'Date' inputs have labels with no `for` association
a11y.rootCause: `<label class="form-label text-black/50">` on lines 56 and 60 have no `for`; the inputs (#dayName line 57, #dayDate line 61) have ids but no matching label association and no `aria-label`. The inputs already carry ids, so the fix is a one-line `for=` add on each label. Not covered by the route-only axe gate (modal isn't opened during the scan).

a11y.title: Home map search input has no accessible name (placeholder is not a label)
a11y.rootCause: `<input id="homeMapSearchInput" type="text" ... placeholder={t('home.searchMapPlaceholder')}>` has no `aria-label` and no associated `<label>`. Placeholder text is not an accessible name (WCAG 4.1.2 / 1.3.1). The adjacent magnifier SVG is `aria-hidden`. The route axe gate scans #home but MapSearchBar renders only inside TripView (requires a seeded trip), so a trip-less fresh-app scan won't flag it.

a11y.title: Map-search results dropdown is not announced (no aria-live / combobox semantics) and offers no arrow-key navigation
a11y.rootCause: `#homeMapSearchResults` (MapSearchBar.tsx:68) is a plain `<div>` with no `role="listbox"`/`aria-live`; mapSearch.ts populates it via `resultsEl.innerHTML` (lines 105, 109) with rows rendered as bare `<button class="map-search-row">` (line 119-128) — no `role="option"`, no `aria-activedescendant`, no aria-expanded on the input, and the input has no keydown handler for ArrowUp/Down/Enter (only an `input` listener + a results-container click handler at line 224). The 'No matches.' message (line 105) is therefore silent to assistive tech.

a11y.title: Day-modal shortlist AM/PM/Eve toggle buttons: no aria-pressed and their labels are overwritten with hardcoded English
a11y.rootCause: The buttons are rendered with `title` only (line 118-123) and no `aria-pressed`, even though they are stateful toggles (the title flips between add/remove). `refreshShortlistButtons()` then sets `btn.textContent = isThere ? '✓ ' + label : label` where `label = time === 'morning' ? '🌅 AM' : ...` (lines 747-748) — hardcoded English, ignoring the localized `_slotLabel`/`t()` strings used elsewhere in the same modal. So the toggle state is invisible to AT, and the i18n'd glyph+label regresses to English on refresh.

a11y.title: LoginWall: button-ready transition is not announced; only the loading/stalled states use aria-live
a11y.rootCause: The loading and stalled hints are `aria-live="polite"` (lines 143, 149), but the 'ready' path (status==='ready', line 78) just unmounts the loading region and injects Google's GIS-rendered button into #loginWallBtnContainer; nothing announces the state change. Removing a live region does not produce an announcement. The freshly rendered Google button also receives no focus.

a11y.title: HeroMap place InfoWindow action buttons/link are hardcoded English ('Add to to-do list', 'View on Google Maps') — not localized
a11y.rootCause: buildInfoWindowHtml() hardcodes the button text on line 288 and the anchor text on line 302 as English string literals instead of using t(). This is the integration seam where a POI/search result is added to the to-do list, so the only label on that action is non-localized.

money-invariant.title: Editing a foreign-currency settlement (name-only companions) silently re-denominates it to the home currency, corrupting the per-currency Settle view
money-invariant.rootCause: openEditSettlementModal's legacy-expense branch is hard-coded to the home currency: the amount input value is convertCurrency(s.euroValue, 'EUR', home) (line 462) and the submit handler sets `s.currency = home` and `s.euroValue = convertCurrency(amount, home, 'EUR')` (lines 486-487), with no currency selector. The original row's currency is discarded. computeTripBalancesByCurrency (balances.ts:253-269) keys strictly on exp.currency+value, so changing the currency moves the settlement to a different per-currency bucket and the original debt re-opens. The server-settlement edit path (openEditServerSettlementModal) correctly preserves `cur = s.currency` (line 534) — only the legacy expense path regresses.

money-invariant.title: Budget stored in a non-EUR currency (via API/legacy) is read as EUR everywhere, producing wrong spent-vs-target status and Insights totals
money-invariant.rootCause: budgets.py validates and persists `currency` + `amount` verbatim (lines 48-49, INSERT line 203) and there is NO normalization to EUR on the server. Every reader, however, assumes amount is EUR: budgetStatus uses `target = budget.amount` while `spent = Σ euroValue` (helpers.ts:196-199); Budgets.tsx renders `formatHome(b.amount, 'EUR')` (line 352); Insights does `convertCurrency(stat.target, 'EUR', targetCurr)` and `convertCurrency(stat.spent, 'EUR', targetCurr)` (Insights.tsx:480-481). budget.currency is read only for the cosmetic 'was X' suffix, never for the math. The expense write path keeps original currency + a real euro_value, but the budget path has no euro_value column and silently treats amount as EUR.

money-invariant.title: Settling the exact foreign-currency debt clears the per-currency Settle view but leaves a residual in the EUR balance / Global tab / Insights (settle-time FX vs frozen expense FX)
money-invariant.rootCause: The suggested debts come from computeTripBalancesByCurrency, which nets the expense's RAW `value` in its own currency (balances.ts:255) and subtracts the settlement's raw `amount` — so settling the exact foreign amount zeroes that bucket. But the EUR balance headline (SettlementView.tsx:175 → computeTripBalances) and the Global/Insights views net the expense's euro_value FROZEN at the expense's write-time rate (balances.ts:183), while the settlement contributes settlement.euroValue (balances.ts:92) computed at settle-time via convertCurrency(amount, currency, 'EUR') (actions.ts:115). When the FX rate moved between expense entry and settlement, frozen-euro_value(expense) != settle-time-euro_value(settlement), so the EUR net does not reach zero even though the foreign amounts match exactly. The two coexisting balance models (raw-currency vs frozen-EUR) disagree by the FX drift.

money-invariant.title: convertCurrency silently falls back to 1:1 for any code missing from both live and static tables, so a stored amount in a now-unsupported currency renders at a fabricated rate
money-invariant.rootCause: _rateFor returns `CONVERSION_RATES[upper] || 1` (currency.ts:77) — the 1.0 fallback is silent. convertCurrency multiplies by this 1:1 rate (currency.ts:119-123), and formatHome (currency.ts:137) and Insights/Settle display callers route through it without first checking hasRate(). The write paths were hardened (expenses C1, settlements S2, budgets IA-10 all gate on get_rate_eur / hasRate), but the DISPLAY-time convertCurrency keeps the legacy 1:1 fallback, so a row that slipped in under the old rules — or whose code later leaves the feed — renders at a fabricated rate. Balance/budget math reads the stored euro_value (safe), but the original-amount and per-currency display hints call convertCurrency directly.

journey-creator-template.title: Template snapshot keeps creator's source:'ai' on marked places — friend's first Accept Plan silently DELETES every imported to-do
journey-creator-template.rootCause: _build_template_snapshot copies the creator's marked_places_json into the snapshot and only runs _clean_marked_places, which pops ONLY dayId and timeOfDay (templates.py:85-109, 182-185). The provenance field `source` is preserved verbatim. Creators build shareable itineraries with the AI planner, so their marked places overwhelmingly carry source:'ai' (stamped by addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified, markedPlaces.ts:320). That source:'ai' is baked into the snapshot, written straight into the friend's new trip by _instantiate_template (templates.py:208-220, which also does NOT strip source), and hydrated into STATE via fetchTripMedia. On the friend's first Accept Plan, onAcceptPlan calls dropAITaggedPlaces(activeTrip) (useAiPlan.ts:459), which deletes EVERY markedPlace whose source==='ai' (markedPlaces.ts:345-348) — i.e. all the imported template to-dos — before adding the friend's own AI items. This is the precise sibling of the recently-fixed bug where promoting a manual to-do to source:'ai' let dropAITaggedPlaces delete it; the template import path re-introduces source:'ai' items the friend never created.

journey-creator-template.title: Imported template to-dos are pre-ticked for AI (forAI:true) — friend's very first AI generation silently includes the creator's places in the prompt
journey-creator-template.rootCause: _clean_marked_places preserves forAI verbatim (only dayId/timeOfDay are popped, templates.py:85-109). useAiPlan builds the to-do prompt suffix from items where p.forAI && p.forManual (useAiPlan.ts:296-298). Imported items carry the creator's forAI:true, so they auto-participate in the friend's first generation with zero user action. This is mild (the to-do page lets the user untick), and arguably intended, but it is an implicit data hand-off the friend didn't choose.

journey-money.title: Batch-import companions are never persisted to the server, so the next /api/data poll silently deletes them (orphaning every imported expense's payer/split names)
journey-money.rootCause: runBatchImport mutates activeTrip.companions in local STATE via addTripCompanion(activeTrip, who) and addTripCompanion(activeTrip, name) for split names (upload.ts:390-398), but it NEVER calls upsertTrip(activeTrip). Its only server calls are upsertExpense per row (persists expenses) and a final syncWithServer() (upload.ts:463). syncWithServer() now POSTs an EMPTY body to /api/sync (api.ts:77-81) — it no longer ships trips/companions at all. So the new companions live only in localStorage. On the next pullFromServer, STATE.trips is fully replaced from the server (api.ts:183 `STATE.trips = allTrips.filter(...)`), merging back only the 4 heavy media fields — companions come straight from the server, which never received the import's additions. Net: imported roster additions are wiped on the next poll while their expenses persist, breaking the expense→companion→settlement hand-off.

journey-money.title: One-click 'Settle' on a no-FX-rate currency debt (ARS/EGP/VND…) fabricates a 1:1 euroValue, poisoning EUR balances, the cross-trip Global tab and Insights with a wildly inflated number
journey-money.rootCause: settleDebt computes `const euroValue = convertCurrency(amount, currency, 'EUR')` (actions.ts:115). For a no-rate currency convertCurrency falls back to rate 1 (currency.ts:71-78 `_rateFor` returns CONVERSION_RATES[code] || 1), so euroValue == the raw foreign amount. PATH A then POSTs {amount, currency:'ARS', euroValue:<raw>}. The server's non-EUR guard (settlements.py:222-228) only rejects when `rate is None AND euro_value is None` — here euro_value is the (bogus) raw number, so it passes; compute_euro_value's cold path returns that client hint verbatim (fx_rates.py:221-228). The settlement is stored with euro_value == raw foreign amount and counts 1:1 as EUR everywhere (applySettlementToBalances euroValue||amount, computeGlobalBalances, settlementEurForTotal, Insights netBalances). SettlementView only consults hasRate(d.currency) to decide whether to show the '≈ EUR' hint (line 302), NOT to gate the Settle button; openManualSettleModal has the same gap.

journey-money.title: History day-total and 'settled' badge use divergent EUR fallbacks for a non-EUR settlement with no euroValue (raw foreign amount vs €0)
journey-money.rootCause: settledStatsForTrip uses settlementEurForTotal (viewData.ts:20-24), which deliberately returns 0 for a non-EUR row with falsy euroValue. But collectSettlementHistory builds each HistoryItem.euroValue as `s.euroValue || s.amount || 0` (viewData.ts:174) — the exact unsafe fallback settlementEurForTotal was written to avoid — and SettlementView's HistoryTab sums those (`totalForDay = items.reduce((s,x)=>s+(x.euroValue||0),0)`, SettlementView.tsx:397). The two surfaces therefore compute the same row's EUR contribution two different ways.

journey-money.title: Batch import splits early rows across a smaller companion roster than later rows, because addTripCompanion grows the roster mid-loop
journey-money.rootCause: Inside the parsedRows.forEach loop, runBatchImport first calls addTripCompanion(activeTrip, who) for the current row (upload.ts:390), then computes the equal-split default from getTripCompanionNames(activeTrip) (upload.ts:404-408). Because the roster is mutated incrementally as rows are processed, tripRoster.length grows during the loop: row 1 (only 'Alice' known) splits 100% to Alice; row 5 (after Alice, Bob, Carol seen) splits 33/33/33. The split denominators are not stable across the import, so equal-share expenses don't reflect the final companion set.

journey-share-clone.title: Share-token clone copies day-plan text even when share_show_plans=0, bypassing the public-page plan gate
journey-share-clone.rootCause: `fetch_share_payload` (public.py:591-622) gates the plan SELECT and the per-day `plan`/`tip` attachment on `show_plans = bool(row['share_show_plans'])` — so a share with `share_show_plans=0` (DEFAULT 0 in database.py:268; share modal defaults `showPlans:false`, share.ts:148) never sends plan text to the viewer. However the clone path does NOT honour that flag: `clone_trip_from_share_token` (trips.py:1515) calls `_clone_trip_record(cursor, row['id'], user_id)` with no privacy argument, and `_clone_trip_record` (trips.py:1384-1425) unconditionally SELECTs `morning, afternoon, evening, tip` and INSERTs them into the clone's trip_days. The clone copies the source rows directly from the DB, ignoring the share_show_plans gate that governs what the recipient was actually shown.

journey-share-clone.title: Clone copies the owner's full markedPlaces wishlist, which the share page and public-trip read both strip from non-members
journey-share-clone.rootCause: `_clone_trip_attempt` (trips.py:1309) copies `src['marked_places_json']` directly into the clone with the comment "copied verbatim (the user's wishlist of places is half the value of cloning)". This deliberate copy contradicts the share/public-trip privacy contract: both read surfaces treat markedPlaces as owner-private. The clone is the only surface that hands the wishlist to a non-member, and it does so unconditionally for both clone entry points.

journey-share-clone.title: Cloned trip's cover image breaks when the source owner revokes the share or deletes the trip
journey-share-clone.rootCause: `_clone_trip_attempt` copies the SOURCE owner's `cover_url` verbatim (trips.py:1319), e.g. `/static/uploads/<ownerA>/abc.jpg`. The clone row is private: `is_archived=0, is_public=0`, no share_token. The serve_upload gate (main.py:1294) can't authorize the cloner: the owner-dir fast-path fails (file is in ownerA's dir, not the cloner's, main.py:1326), and the member-join requires BOTH the caller and the file's owner to be accepted members of a trip referencing the file (main.py:1364-1378) — but ownerA is not a member of the clone, and the cloner is not a member of the source. The only thing keeping the cover visible is the anonymous public-cover fallback (main.py:1448-1456), which matches `cover_url` only on trips where `is_public=1 OR share_token IS NOT NULL`. That row is the SOURCE trip. So the clone's cover lives or dies by the source's share/public state: revoke the share link (revoke_share_link sets share_token=NULL, trips.py:1233) or unpublish, and the clone's cover stops resolving. Delete the source trip and delete_upload_files removes the physical file (trips.py:347/455), 404ing it forever.

journey-media-pdf.title: EXIF-tagged photos render TWICE in the PDF: once in the day card, once in the trip-wide Photos gallery
journey-media-pdf.rootCause: The route merges trip-level photos_json entries that carry a `dayId` into that day's `photos` column so the day card embeds them (__init__.py:1076-1089), then renders the day card via `_collect_photos(day['photos'])` (line 533). Separately, the trip-wide gallery calls `_collect_photos(trip_photos, limit=_PHOTO_MAX_PER_TRIP)` over the FULL, UNFILTERED `photos_json` list (line 893; `trip_photos = _safe_json(trip_row.get('photos_json'), [])` at line 251). The gallery never excludes the day-tagged photos already inlined into day cards. The docstring at line 887-888 explicitly claims the gallery shows 'any per-day photos not already inlined in the day cards', but the code does no such filtering, and `_collect_photos`/`_photo_grid` perform no dedup. Photos are routinely day-tagged because the Photos-tab upload path EXIF-matches each file to a day and calls `addTripPhoto(trip, { src, dayId })` (tripMediaModals.ts:425-429).

journey-media-pdf.title: Uploaded documents (boarding passes / bookings) are never included in the exported PDF and have no export toggle
journey-media-pdf.rootCause: The PDF builder `_build_trip_pdf` has sections for days, to-dos, budgets, expenses, settlements, companions, marked places, and photos — but NONE for documents. The export route's trip SELECT (`__init__.py:1014-1016`) never fetches `documents_json`, and `trip.documents` / day `tickets` are never read in the builder. The i18n string table (`_i18n.py`) has no `sec_documents` key. The export modal (`modals/pdf.ts:62-87`) renders an `includePhotos` checkbox but no documents checkbox. So documents — a first-class media type with their own write path and Documents UI tab — are structurally unrepresented in the export.

journey-media-pdf.title: Overview-map header 'EVERY DAY ON ONE MAP · N PINS' is hardcoded English in every localized PDF export
journey-media-pdf.rootCause: The overview-pins map header at `__init__.py:505` is an f-string literal `f"EVERY DAY ON ONE MAP   ·   {len(pins)} PIN{'S' if len(pins) != 1 else ''}"` rather than a `tr(...)` lookup. There is no corresponding key in `_STRINGS` in `_i18n.py`. This path is reached on the default export (includeDays + includeDayPins default True) for any trip whose days have coordinates. The PDF-5 i18n pass localized section titles and slot labels but missed this header (and the 'S' pluralization is English-only).
